#### form of the Lord, imposter incarnations, spiritual guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=294
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To defeat all those atheistic arguments, Kunti Devi is presenting a valid evidence here, and she is describing the Lord as that personality."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the atheistic argument that the Lord has no form, highlighting Kunti Devi's description of the Lord's features and emphasizing the importance of distinguishing genuine incarnations from imposters through guru, sadhu, and shastra.

She is revealing to us how the Lord looks like. Sometimes, many times we come across people saying, the Lord has no form. So, he is Nirakar. So, to defeat all those atheistic arguments, Kunti Devi is presenting a valid evidence here, and she is describing the Lord as that personality, who has Pankaj. Ananda can identification foremost as Resarchajanabha whose navel is lotus... has a lotus like.. depression and Nama Pankajamaline he is always adorned with garlands of lotus flowers. Nama Pankaja Netaraya his eyes are lotus-shaped eyes and Namaste Pankajangraye he, whose feet is marked with the symbol of lotus flower. So Kuntideva is giving us the evidence that how Lord actually looked like. So here there are very nice points that Srila Prabhupada delineates for all of our understanding. Let us go one by one. The first point that Prabhupada says in the purport is he is giving us an idea that when the Supreme Lord is there, he has some specific insignia, some specific signs that distinguish him, that distinguishes him from the people, ordinary people. Although he may come down as one amongst us, Prabhupada says, but still he has specific symptoms or specific insignia. And those are the things which will help us understand whether somebody is a Lord or not. Especially in the today's world. We see that very few people are interested in spirituality. So most of them are running like a madman behind sense gratification. But amongst those people who are little interested in spirituality, most of them are misguided. And we all come across very frequently that so and so God has appeared in so and so place, so and so God-man. And if you keep reading newspapers, every night, now and then you hear a caption self-styled God-man trapped, self-styled God-man exposed. So what is happening is the faith in the devotees who are genuinely seeking the mercy of the Lord, who wants to understand the nature of the Lord, they are demotivated. They are discouraged to continue their pursuit of spirituality. So it is. It is very important for us to know this, have this knowledge of how do we distinguish an imposter incarnation from that of the real incarnation. This is a very important knowledge that we all need to possess as devotees.

#### scriptural authority, identifying imposters, Kalki
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=481
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should always consult, Prabhupada says, three things to have this knowledge of who is an imposter and who is the real incarnation."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of consulting guru, sadhu, and shastra to discern real incarnations from imposters, using the example of Kalki to illustrate how scriptures provide precise details about future events.

For example, if you take the shastras, the first thing is the shastras. We should always consult, Prabhupada says, three things to have this knowledge of who is an imposter and who is the real incarnation. One is Guru. We should hear from the Guru. Second is Sadhu or the saintly people. And the third is Shastra. All these three things should be in unison. It cannot be that one is contradicting the other. For example, we sometimes hear of somebody by name Kalki has appeared now. So Bhagawan Kalki is there. Then many people, thousands of people go and maybe he is charging some amount to give his darshan. And maybe if your question has to be answered, there is a little more amount. And if you have to see him, there is a little more amount. And if you have to see him, there is a little more amount. And if you have to see him personally, it is little more fair. So this is the incarnation that we all come across. But shastras are so precise that they give us the information of when this Kalki incarnation is going to happen. Bhagavatam says that the Kali Yuga's life span is 4,32,000 years. Of which we have just passed 5,000 years. That means 4,27,00,000 years yet to go. At the end of Kali Yuga, that means after 4,27,00,000 years, during the conjunction between Kali Yuga and Satya Yuga, Lord Kalki appears. And he destroys all the miscreants and then leads to the fresh cycle of Satya Yuga. And the shastras are so precise that they even pinpoint the location at which Lord Kalki is going to appear. Bhagavatam says Lord Kalki will appear in a place called Sambhalpur. And it also gives us the information on his father's name is Vishnu Joshi. Just see, what is going to happen 4,27,00,000 years ahead of time is precisely foretold as to the father's name, location, etc. This is the verdict of shastras. So, if we come across any person claiming himself to be God or sent by God, the first thing we should do is cross-check with the scriptures. His incarnation should be corroborated by the scriptures. Then we can accept, otherwise not.

---
#### false gurus, deception, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=783
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada says that this is the story of a blue jackal who wanted to become the jungle ka raja."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of the blue jackal to illustrate how false gurus exploit innocent people in the name of spirituality, driven by their inability to restrain from sense gratification.

Prabhupada tells this story and he tells this is a blue jackal story. So, in Bengali, in Bengal, there is one, this famous story which Prabhupada tells to all his disciples during one class. He says that once a jackal, we all know jackal for its cunning mental, cunning mind and how it tricks the fellow animals and things like that. So, once it so happened that this jackal once was passing by the forest and it accidentally fell into a tub. Into a tub of blue dye. The tub was consisting of blue dye. And as soon as the jackal fell into the tub, the whole body was completely changed to the bluish hue. So, nobody could identify that it is a jackal. So, now jackal when it learnt that this whole body has been changed including the face and everything. So, because as it is, it is a cunning animal, it wanted to exploit the fellow animals. So, the jackal with the bluish hue all around its body now comes to the forest and seeing this very rare species which none of the animals had seen. So, they come and question this jackal, where are you from? Who are you? So, then this blue jackal intelligently replies, I have been sent by God. And have come here on behalf of God. So, then as soon as the fellow animals hear this statement from the jackal, they worship him and they give him all the awe and reverence and then they provide him with all the kinds of foodstuffs. So, the jackal felt its purpose is accomplished. So, then it went on for a few days and the message went to the king of the forest. And the lion of the forest also came to see the messenger of God, to see the God's representative, God's own representative. And it also offered its respects and it also got some special food ordered for the jackal. And as the things were going on, on one particular night, there were some other jackals, they started howling. You all would have heard the howling of jackals, right? In fact, Prabhupada, when he was describing this story, he says, Wah! Wah! Jackal started howling. So, and one specific quality of jackal is that when other jackals are howling, calling for something, they can't restrain themselves from responding to the call. So, when the jackal started howling, other jackals started howling, this blue jackal also responded the call by loudly howling. And that's the point. And that's the point where it was exposed. So, Prabhupada says that this is the story of a blue jackal who wanted to become the jungle ka raja. He wanted to be the king of jackal, sorry, king of the forest and it had exploited the sentiments of the poor animals in the forest. And he further says that this is how people in the modern age, in the name of spirituality, are trying to exploit, some innocent people. They claim themselves to be sent by God and they devise their own methodologies but they can't restrain themselves from responding to the calls of nature. That is how you see many times the fake Babas are exposed when they are caught with, you know, so many other scams and with money, women. So, all these are happening. Why? Because they are not really transcended the urges of the senses. Artificially, they wanted to go to a transcendental position and claim themselves to be superior to the others, masses. Why? Because they can exploit their sentiments and their sense gratification can be achieved in a quite jubilant way. It's quite obvious that when a person dresses himself in saffron robes, dresses himself as a devotee, naturally, some innocent, simple people, they come, they offer the respects, they offer the riches, they offer various kinds of wealth. So, all these are the things that these fake people are looking for. So, they can't themselves restrain from the calls of the sense gratification, just like this jackal in bluish hue, bluish color, could not restrain itself from the howling calls of the fellow jackals.

#### true religion, love of God, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=1347
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Our religion is considered a true religion if it is teaching you how to love God."
**Summary:** This segment defines true religion as that which teaches love of God and provides access to the order of God, emphasizing surrender as the ultimate instruction.

So, Prabhupada explains that religion means to love God. Forget about today we are trying to fight in the names of communism, all that I am a Hindu, you are a Muslim, he is a Christian. So, Prabhupada says, doesn't matter which religion, material religion that we are ascribed to, our religion is considered a true religion if it is teaching you how to love God. So, that is the proof of a true religion. And further, Srimad Bhagavatam says, dharmam tu sakshat bhagavat pranitam. What is the meaning of dharma? Dharma is that thing which will teach you and which will give you the access to the order of God. Dharmam tu sakshat bhagavat pranitam. Pranitam means order. Order or instruction. So, a real religion is that it should tell you what the Lord wants from you, what is the Lord expecting from you, how are we supposed to conduct our activities in this world. So, when we get that access to that information, that is the real religion. Further, Bhagavad Gita also says, one of the supreme most instruction of all the instructions that Bhagavad Gita gives is towards the end of Bhagavad Gita where Krishna says, sarva dharman parityajya mam ekam sharanam rajah. Give up all your give up all other dharmas and just surrender unto me. And it is at that point where Srimad Bhagavatam begins. Srimad Bhagavatam says, dharma projita kaitavotra paramo nirmatsaranam satam. Bhagavatam is telling, this is the literature where it has the dharma is presented in its purest form because all the cheating type of religions, cheating type of philosophies are kicked out of this book. Dharma projita kaitavotra and it is paramo nirmatsaranam satam. It is highly, you know, especially transcendental literature meant for the great souls.

---
#### real dharma, surrender to God, unmotivated devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=1474
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you are progressively moving towards surrendering unto the God, then that is real dharma."
**Summary:** This segment defines real dharma as that which helps one progress in surrendering to God in an unmotivated and unconditional way, highlighting the importance of enduring through difficult situations without losing faith.

So, these are some of the indications to understand what is the meaning of real dharma. And further, Bhagavatam also says, savai pumsam paro dharmo yato bhaktir adhokshaje. So, what is the meaning of real dharma? If at all, if you are saying that you are following real dharma, process of real dharma, then it should help you go further in your surrender unto God. If you are progressively moving towards surrendering unto the God, then that is real dharma. And how should that surrender be? Bhagavatam explains, ahaituki apratihata. That should be unmotivated and unconditional. Unmotivated means, we should not have any motivation in coming to, ah, coming and, coming to a temple or serving the Lord or doing devotional service. And it should also, uninterrupted. Or sometimes, you know, we also see some business people, they have a understanding with the Lord. Now, if I do this business, if I get this much, I'll give you this much. Or there are some traditional families who will say, mera beta ka shaadi nahi horai. So, if a child gets married, then I will give this to you. So, many times our devotional prayers are motivated. And sometimes it is also interrupted. When things are going smooth, we'll all be fine. When the reversals start happening, when there are some difficult situations in our life, at that time, we will give up devotional service. Like Prabhupada gives the example of when there was World War II, there were many people who had sent, many women especially, who had sent their husbands, their brothers, their children to the war. And they were all great devotees of the Lord. They had faith that they will come back. But then it so happened that most of them lost their lives. So, these women back at the homes who were expecting their near and dear ones, their kith and kin will return, they became turned into atheists. Because they were expecting them to return safe, but that didn't happen. So, when some reversals in life will happen to us, when things are not going as per the plan that we have designed for, that we have thought of, then we again give up devotional service. But Bhagavatam says, that Dharma which teaches you how to surrender unto the Lord, and that surrender should be ahaituki and apratihata. It should be unmotivated and uninterrupted. We should not have any desires behind doing our service, and at the same time, we should also be enduring, willing to endure through the difficult situations in our life without giving up the faith on the Lord. So, when such things happen, then that is called real Dharma.

#### humility, devotion, qualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=1648
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kunti Devi is being a very great ardent devotee of the Lord, is claiming herself to be unfit, to be unfit to see the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the humility of great devotees like Kunti Devi, who consider themselves unfit to see the Lord, with the challenging attitude of those who demand to see God without qualification.

The next point Prabhupada explains in this purport is he is saying that Kunti Devi is being a very great ardent devotee of the Lord, is claiming herself to be unfit, to be unfit to see the Lord. Kunti Devi is telling, I am not fit to see you, O my Lord. So actually, this is nothing but the humility of great devotees. She is putting herself into the roles of the common men like us, and she is telling that I am unfit to see the Lord. On the other side, we come across in the world around us, when we go and talk to our friends, talk to our relatives, talk to the people who visit us, who visit the temple about God, they ask us, sometimes people get into an argument, can you show me God? See, on the one side, we are seeing great devotees that I am not qualified to see you, O my Lord. She is praying to Lord Krishna, who is standing right in front of her. But on the other side, we, out of illusion, we challenge the Lord, or we challenge the devotees that can you show me God? If God exists, can you show me?

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "First, deserve, and then desire."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the faulty argument of demanding to see God, emphasizing the need for qualification, the limitations of imperfect senses, and the understanding that the Lord is not an order carrier.

So actually, Prabhupada defeats this kind of faulty arguments with many such answers. First of all, Prabhupada says, if somebody asks us, can you show me God? First of all, we should ask them another question in return, in reply, are we qualified to see God? There is a principle in the mainstream world, first, deserve, and then desire. We should first of all, achieve the qualification. We should be deserving to see the Lord. Then we should desire to see the Lord. Desiring without deserving, it's a fallacy. It will not happen. Now today we all read of prime minister of the country, his movements, his activities, his things in the parliament. We hear of so many activities in the news, paper, video clips, etcetera, etcetera. So now if I ask you, does prime minister exist? Obviously yes. I have been seeing, I have been hearing of him. So then I challenge you, can you take me to him? You cannot show me by picture, because then I will, I have Krishna's picture with me, I will also show you. So then no, no, I can show you his audio clips. Then Lord's audio is also there, Bhagavad Gita. Bhagavad Gita is nothing but the song sung by the Lord. The activities of the Lord are described in Srimad Bhagavatam. So you cannot say no, prime minister is moving here and there. So that's the proof, no. So show me prime minister, take me, take me to his, his personal things you should show me, his personal form, you should make me meet him. So he will say no I can't. So why? Because he is not qualified. Now when this is our situation, when we hear from the Shastras, this country is a small portion of this world, on this planet of many countries. And this planet is one small thing in the system galaxy of our nine planets. And our whole universal system, it's again a dot in the universal manifestation. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explains that our universe is like a mustard seed in a bag of mustard seeds. When you have a bag of mustard seeds, how much will one mustard seed be? So in that. So when we are not qualified to meet the representative of a small nation, then how, what, what can be our audacity to challenge the existence of God who is the creator. As this verse is explaining, when Lord lies down as Garbhodakshaya Vishnu, lotus is grown from his navel and on the stem of the lotus where all the planetary systems are situated, on the top of the lotus, Lord Brahma is born. And we are challenging the existence of the Lord. We want to see him face to face. So this is the first argument that Prabhupada gives. Ask them, are we first of all qualified to see God? And the second thing he says that the Lord is not our order carrier. Just like we all have, some of us have maid servant or some servant at our office, our assistant. So he is available at our call. So we call him, then he comes running and folds his hands and waits for our order. So now when we are desiring, Krishna I want to see you. Krishna is not our order carrier. He is not our servant to just come running on our call. So therefore, it is imperative to understand that we have to qualify. And the third thing is we all have imperfect senses. Our senses are not perfect. Our eyes has a limited vision. All our senses, we have a limited sense. So how can we see the Lord who is beyond matter with these imperfect senses? So it is not possible.

---
#### devotion, love, capturing the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=1980
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You can purchase the Lord, you can capture the Lord who is uncaptured, incapturable only by love."
**Summary:** This segment explains that while we cannot see the Lord with imperfect senses, we can qualify ourselves through devotion and love, as demonstrated by Arjuna and other pastimes.

So then that means one may ask a question. So I cannot see the Lord. Forever? No. As Prabhupada is explaining, we have to qualify to see the Lord. For example, Krishna acted as Parthasarati, charioteer for Arjuna. Now we are discussing a point where Krishna cannot be our order carrier and here he is exactly doing the opposite. He is carrying out the orders of Arjuna. He has become the charioteer of Arjuna. Why? Because Arjuna had qualified himself by devotion. And there are many other pastimes in Bhagavatam. Every other pastime, if you see, these are the common, the common denominator is you can purchase the Lord, you can capture the Lord who is uncaptured, incapturable only by love.

---
#### Damodara, devotion, love of God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=2034
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Fear personified is afraid of Krishna. And this Krishna is afraid of mother Yashoda's small stick in her hand. That's the beauty of devotion."
**Summary:** This segment uses the Damodara pastime to illustrate how Krishna, despite being the Supreme Personality of Godhead, can be captured by the devotion and love of His devotees, like Mother Yashoda.

And like from today, we are starting the Damodar Mass, Karthik Mass. As you all are aware of the pastime of Damodara, the very instructive point that this pastime of Damodara, which is suggesting us, is teaching us is, although Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead, He was captured by His mother Yashoda because of her devotion to the Lord. You all know that pastime how Krishna was, had become naughty. Krishna was being fed by His mother Yashoda. And Yashoda realized that there was a milk pot which was overflowing. And so while she was feeding Krishna, she put aside him and ran towards the milk pot to stop it overflowing. And Krishna became very angry and he hit some pots and the pots were flowed and the curds and milk were all spilled all around the floor. And when mother Yashoda came back, so she saw all these things. So she became angry on Krishna. So she wanted to capture Krishna. And Bhagavatam very nicely describes that Krishna started running and mother Yashoda started chasing Krishna. And Bhagavatam describes that Krishna is known as fear personified. Sorry, Krishna is feared by even the fear personified. We are fearful of so many things in the world. So if you take something as fear personified, even that fear personified, he is fearful of Krishna. That Krishna is fearful of mother Yashoda's stick. Just see the contradiction. So it is not that, you know, Krishna is ordinary. Fear personified is afraid of Krishna. And this Krishna is afraid of mother Yashoda's small stick in her hand. That's the beauty of devotion. So when mother Yashoda started chasing Krishna, with her stick, Krishna started running and she could not capture him. After some time, when Lord Krishna realized that my mother has become tired of trying to catch me, he allows himself to be captured. And the story doesn't end there. The past time doesn't end there. Further, when mother Yashoda caught him, she tried to bind him with a rope. And when she tried to bind him with a rope, she felt, she realized that the rope fell short by two inches. So she collected some more ropes from all around the house and joined those ropes, thread to this existing rope and tried to bind. The rope again fell short by two inches. So she collected all that was there in her home and joined. Still it fell short by two inches. Now again when Krishna realized that my mother is trying so severely, intensely, let me not trouble her more, he allows himself to be bound. So if you see in this past time, first of all, he is running off the fear of mother Yashoda's stick and he gets captured. Mother Yashoda will never be able to capture Krishna. But because of her devotion, she catches him. Then she binds him.

So similarly Bhagavatam says, we can also go to that stage. It is not that everything is in air. No, you will see one day Lord, Lord. You cannot see the Lord. No, Bhagavatam says, When our eyes are anointed with love of God, when that Krishna Bhakti is awakened within our heart, then this Supreme Lord, Shri Krishna becomes captured just like he was captured by the devotion of Mother Yashoda. So this is one understanding that we get that how we can qualify ourselves to see the Lord face to face. But somehow, although we have all this information and this is all these are possible, we don't realize the importance and the boon that we have got in this life. And we still go back to our own habits. In this direction, Srila Prabhupada tells us a very instructive story and which when I heard, I felt it is true with all of us, especially the practicing sadhakas. See there are lots of hundreds and thousands of people, lakhs of people who are not aware of such a knowledge, instructions of Gita, the greatness of Srila Prabhupada's teachings. So let's leave them for this discussion. Let's discuss about all of us who are so fortunate to have come across the teachings of Srila Prabhupada, Gita and his organization, etc.

#### attachment, material desires, spiritual gift
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H9JkWxXdI3w?t=2352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are so unfortunate that you could not experience a royal life, you could not experience the royalty of the robes that you are wearing because of your attachment to that old rags with your bag."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of the beggar with old rags to illustrate how devotees, despite receiving the spiritual gift of chanting Hare Krishna, often remain attached to material desires and fail to fully experience the benefits of their spiritual practice.

So Prabhupada tells us a very instructive story about our sadhakas, our state of sadhakas. So Prabhupada says, it so happened that once in a kingdom there was a huge palace and there was a beggar who used to everyday pass by that palace to do his profession of begging. It so happened that once while passing through that palace he came across a small notice in front of the palace door which read that those who can dress themselves, dress themselves up in royal robes can come for a banquet dining with the king the next evening. So on seeing this notice this beggar started thinking, Oh my God! Such a nice offer! Those who can dress themselves in royal robes, in rich garments, they are given an invitation to dine with the king. So but then how unfortunate I am, I don't have any royal dress. All that I have is this torn clothes. It is also known as racks. So he was feeling very desperate about going to the dining with the king. So he started thinking but then he felt, no that is not possible. Then he started moving away. But then after walking for a certain point of time, he realized something and then came running back to the palace door. And then he requests the doorman for an appointment with the king. So the doorman were quite surprised seeing the dressing, shabbily dressed beggar. But then still as a matter of duty they go to the king and then give the information. And to his fortune, the beggar was given an appointment. And the beggar goes in and meets the king. And then on his inquiry, this beggar requests the king that oh king, I came to know about the party that you are hosted for tomorrow evening and you have mentioned a criteria that people should dress themselves in the rich robes, the costly robes. But then I am so interested, so eager to attend that dining with all the able men, especially with your good self. But I am not qualified because I don't have that kind of clothes. If I can dare enough, can I request from you to donate a pair of old clothes that you were wearing earlier so that I become qualified to come and attend the dining with you? So the king was for a moment he was taken aback seeing the guts of this beggar to come and request for the robes. Therefore become qualified and all that. But then he still appreciated him and then he will call for his prince and he will order the prince that you please give him one set of your dress so that he can come tomorrow and dine with all the others. So accordingly, the prince takes this old man to his room and gives him one good set of clothes to wear and so the beggar will remove all his rags and then pack into a bag old bag and then he wears those royal robes and after having adorned those robes unto himself the prince says O beggar you are now qualified to come tomorrow and you may not need these rags anymore these stone clothes anymore because the dress that I have given you can never wear out in this life. That is a special dress it can never get torn it will never wear out so you can be happy eternally for this whole life you can enjoy wearing this dress it will never get dirty it will never get worn it will never get torn and you can be very happy and you never need these old clothes he will tell that and he will send off the beggar so the beggar comes back to his home he was quite suspicious about that prince's statement that these clothes will never wear out or tear out so the next day the time arrives when the dining time is announced by the king so this beggar after having worn that royal robes still he will carry that old tone clothes rags in his old tone bag and he keeps that in his arms and he goes to the assembly why? because he is always afraid if something happens to this dress if something unfortunate happens then I will need a dress or maybe if I am not comfortable at some point of time so some kind of apprehensions he had he carried that and when he goes there he was invited to the dining and while he was dining he had some old rags on his lap because he had kept on his lap that bag was time and again falling down so every time he used to keep them after some time it will fall down so when it falls down he tries to take that bag by that time some delicacies were served there were hundreds of delicacies served in that dinner so every time the beggar was losing his bag the bag was falling down he was trying to take that and adjust himself so he was feeling very bad what is this I should not have carried he was feeling like that but then the assembly over and then he came out and the life continued but it became a unique characteristic of this beggar that wherever he went he carried this bag of old rags old clothes why

### Srimad Bhagavatam I HG Kulashekara Chaitanya Dasa I SB 1.8.20 I 02.10.2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### surrender, humility, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=718
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So we have to pray to the Lord always every time."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of humility and prayer in spiritual life, contrasting Krishna's entry into the Bhagavatam as a simple chariot driver with the expected virat roopa, and emphasizing that pride hinders spiritual progress.

And in the 7th chapter, one may expect Krishna to be a virat roop, where thousands of hairs, thousands of hands, thousands of people falling at his feet, praying, sastra, shesha, purushaha, sastraksha, sastrapada. But surprisingly, we see some different picture there. So this, I will tell you that how Krishna entered Bhagavatam scene, scene of Bhagavatam or entered the stage of Bhagavatam. Actually literally it is a, Bhagavatam is a continuation of Bhagavad Gita. One may expect Krishna is how Vyasadeva is introducing Krishna as virat roopa. No. Krishna enters the stage of Bhagavatam as a simple chariot driver. And Arjuna has taken shelter of Krishna, asking him to protect him from the onslaught of Ashwatthama's brahmastra weapon. We all have heard in the previous classes. So there in Bhagavad Gita, finally after Bhagavad Gita, he is saying that I will surrender unto you, surrender to your will. So here is surrender to Krishna and Krishna is first enters the scene of Bhagavatam by being a chariot driver. So we have to see through the eyes of Srimad Bhagavatam that this prayers like Kuntimane prayers and Arjuna, Arjuna also praying, paahi paahi, what is that? That is one of the slokas in Bhagavatam. So this all prayers are so powerful, which makes our heart very free from all material pangs. Because if we don't start praying to God with all humility, then our, slowly our false pride start building up and the friend of false pride is false ego. The ego walls start growing thicker and thicker and thicker and the message of Godhead will not go inside our heart and transform us.

#### submission, rationality, false pride
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=847
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So unless and until you don't develop the mood of submission to the Supreme Almighty God in terms of prayer, that is nothing but giving thanksgiving, thank you my Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an anecdote about visiting professors and scholars who, due to their rationalistic and socialistic beliefs, refuse to bow down or pray before Lord Narsingadev, illustrating the difficulty of dissolving false pride without submission to God.

From past few months I have been visiting many professors and many scholars and many poets. It so happens, it is very hard, sometimes it is a hard time for me to bring this people to the temple. Somewhat of great hardship when I bring them to the temple and when I take them to in the front of Lord Narsingadev and I tell them the pastime of Narsimha and all those pastimes. And finally I bow down in the front of Lord Narsimha so that they follow me or at least pray and not even an inch, they budge from moving, you know, not praying or folding hands. Then one such time I ask them, they said, no, no, we are leftist, we are the rationals, we are socialist. Going to the temple and going to the mall are not different. In the mall there is a doll and decorated with jewellery and dress and here is an idol decorated with jewellery and dress. So unless and until you don't develop the mood of submission to the Supreme Almighty God in terms of prayer, that is nothing but giving thanksgiving, thank you my Lord. There is a prayer.

#### humility, ego, accomplishments
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=927
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So similarly, all of our accolades, all of our accomplishments, all of our achievements, discoveries, which we do in our life, all are like this."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of dog footprints on a cement road, which are often overlooked or trampled upon, to illustrate how people take pride in their abilities and achievements, leading to ego and hindering surrender to Krishna.

One doesn't get their false pride dissolved, cleared. Just below this hall in front of Multi-Ocean Theatre and Open Air Theatre, we have this road, there is a cement road. I don't know how many of you have noticed it or not. There are some impressions of dog on that cement. How many of you have seen? Good, at least three of you. So that probably 20 years back when this road was paved with that fresh cement, dog might have passed through that and impression has been created. There is a footprint created on that. And I was just thinking how many of us are looking at that? How many of us are praising it? Most of us who stamp on it or spit on it and go without noticing it. So similarly, all of our accolades, all of our accomplishments, all of our achievements, discoveries, which we do in our life, all are like this. We, you know, people out of great pride and out of great, they take pride in their abilities, in their talents, in their achievements. They want some appreciations from people. And this is how their ego walls start. Growing more thicker and thicker. That's how they never surrender to Krishna.

Just like Kuntimani through her prayers, in these prayers. And these chapters are so powerful that it can awaken one. It can awaken one's dormant love for Krishna. So here, so let's recap and summarize what was discussed in the previous classes. Just at the end of seventh chapter, we all heard that Pandavas, after finishing their final rites of their dead sons and other relatives. What is that? Putra Shoka Turaha Sarve Pandavaha Sahakrishnaya. So having sufficiently lamented because of death of their relatives. So they went, they and Krishna along with Krishna, they went to the banks of the Ganges and they took bath. As soon as they took bath in the river Ganges. They all felt a sense of great satisfaction or as Gandharva says, relief within them. A freedom from material pangs. Why? Because Sukadeva Swami says, because the waters had touched the feet of lotus feet of Krishna. So after that Krishna sufficiently. Consoles them. Pacifies them. And he comes near Queen Kunti and bow down to Queen Kunti, taking her permission to return to Dwarka. That supreme personality of Godhead, which has been explained in Bhagavad Gita. He is the foremost of all Devatas. He is the seeding father of all living entities. Everything rests upon him. He is the master of all living entities, protector of all living entities, maintainer of all living entities. That supreme personality of Godhead is falling at the feet of Kunti Maharani. Just imagine the exalted position of Kunti Maharani. That supreme personality is falling to show an example. Being an example to the society that the social etiquette which we have to follow. Because activities of Krishna, every activity of Krishna has some significance.

#### protection, surrender, Krishna's mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=1173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nothing will come to protect us at the point of our death. This example is Draupadi. Uttara."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the stories of Uttara and Draupadi seeking protection from Krishna, highlighting the futility of relying on material relationships and the importance of surrendering to Krishna's mercy in times of distress.

So after that Krishna prepared himself to depart to Dwarka. So he went to his chariot. And he was about to get on his chariot. Immediately it so happened, Uttara, the daughter-in-law of Draupadi. From the far distance. She ran so quickly, hurryingly towards Krishna's feet. She caught hold of Krishna's feet very tightly. And started praying him out of great fear. That Bhagavatam says, Uttaraam bhaya vihvalaam. With great fear. What she prayed? Pahi pahi maha yogin. Deva Deva Jagat Pate. She is praying to Krishna. My Lord Krishna, please protect me. Accept you. No one is competent enough to protect me from this imminent death. Actually even Kunti is, so Uttara is following in the footsteps of Draupadi. When just imagine when Draupadi was there all alone. In the assembly. And all great generals are there in front of her. Bhishma, Drona, Karuna, Krupa. So many great generals. And learned in Dharmic principles. Scholars. Erudite scholars. And her own five husbands. Yudhishthira who is the protector of Dharma. When none of them came to protect her. When she tried her hard best to, from her strength to protect herself from the onslaught of Dushasana's atrocities. Finally she raised her hand and said, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare. Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Just imagine that situation when you are there. You are so humiliated. Your own husbands are not coming to help me. Asatsabha is saying. Bhagavatam further it says. That asatsabha, similarly it is asatsainya which we have. Dehapatrakalata, atmasainya is asatsapi. We think our wife will protect us. Children will protect us. Children will protect us. Family, husband, police, our law will protect us. Nothing will come to protect us at the point of our death. This example is Draupadi. Uttara. So she is saying, Pai Pai Mahayogin. Here I got one verse from Sabha Parva, Mahabharata. She says, Govinda Dwaraka Vasin, Krishna Gopi Janapriya, Krishna Krishna Mahayogin, Vishwatma Vishwabhavana. She is praying Krishna Krishna Mahayogin. Here Uttara is saying, Pai Pai Mahayogin. You are the greatest mystic.

#### mysticism, Krishna's protection, Kunti's prayers
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=1338
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just imagine that situation when you are there. You are so humiliated. Your own husbands are not coming to help me. Asatsabha is saying. Bhagavatam further it says. That asatsabha, similarly it is asatsainya which we have. Dehapatrakalata, atmasainya is asatsapi. We think our wife will protect us. Children will protect us. Children will protect us. Family, husband, police, our law will protect us. Nothing will come to protect us at the point of our death. This example is Draupadi. Uttara."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the stories of Uttara and Draupadi seeking protection from Krishna, highlighting the futility of relying on material relationships and the importance of surrendering to Krishna's mercy in times of distress.

Through mysticism, please protect me from the imminent death of my unborn son in my womb. So Krishna through his plenary expansion, as Paramatma was present in all of our hearts, he entered the womb of Uttara and protected from the radiations of Brahmastra and neutralized it. And she says, Brahma tejo vinirmukta hi. So Krishna, so here finally again Krishna pacified all of them. And he prepared himself to then finally offered a bed farewell to everyone and made pranams, final pranams to all of them. And he was about to, he got onto the chariot. He was about to move towards Dwaraka. And all the residents of Astinapur were assembled there. And the Pandavas and all the relatives of Pandavas. They were so much overwhelmed by the grief, the pain, the sorrow of Krishna leaving them. It's as if they were feeling that life air in them has been leaving now. And they just remained the dead bodies thereafter. Because this Astinapur, residents of Astinapur had practically witnessed Krishna being a chariot driver, Krishna being a guard, Krishna being their friend, Krishna being their intimate friend. Krishna being their intimate relative has protected Pandavas right from their childhood. And not only that, Krishna is so kind, compassionate and so benevolent to all living entities. And that Supreme Personality of Godhead is living all of them. And they all felt a sense of great pain within them. And Queen Kunti was the foremost of Kauravas at that time. She swiftly moved towards Krishna and caught hold of Krishna's hand. And she started praying. These are their prayers. These are wonderful prayers. So in these 26 verses, the first 3 verses, Kunti Maharani explains about the philosophy or the truth of Krishna. Truth of absolute truth, absolute nature of Krishna. And the next 2 verses, she speaks about the quality of Krishna. And further after it, Kunti Maharani explains about the glorious activities of Krishna.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces Kunti's prayers and emphasizes the importance of surrender in bhakti yoga, contrasting it with karma yoga, and explaining Krishna's nature as Adipurusha, beyond material nature and influences.

So someone can get an opportunity to do some postgraduate studies on Kunti Maharani prayers. It will be a great service to Bhagavatam. So, so much things to learn from that. So the first verse starts with, Namaste Purusham Dvadhyam Namaste Purusham Dvadhyam Namaste means she is completely sundered. She is completely sundered. Sharanagati, prapatti. Prabhupada says, one of the purpose, the difference between the bhakti yoga and karma yoga. In a bhakti yoga, first you have to surrender, then you have to start working. But in the karma yoga, karma nama… The first you start working, then you offer the results to Krishna. So in a safer side, better surrender to Krishna even if some, if you are not properly performing your work, you will be saved. Otherwise, we have to face the consequence of bad karmas. we have to face the consequence of bad karma, sinful activities. So, this is very much of, what is that? Namaste Purusham Tardhyam. You are the Purusha, you are the man. What is Adipurusha, Adhyam, and not equal to some Indra, Chandra, Eshwaram, is the control of all planets, of everything. Prakrte Param, who is beyond or who doesn't come under the purview of material nature, or who is unaffected by the influences or qualities of material world. Prakrte Param, Alaksham Sarva Bhutanam, Alaksham is not visible to the living entities, but Prabhupada mentions in the Parapara.

#### rebelliousness, Maya, sense perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=1607
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains why Krishna is not visible to rebellious souls, attributing it to their ignorance and bewilderment by Maya, and emphasizing that Krishna is beyond sense perception.

The rebellious souls. If at all we are not able to see Krishna, it's only because we are rebellious. We are rebellious to Krishna. We rebelled against Krishna's will. We have something separate desire, separate from Krishna, away from Krishna. So, then, Antarbahir Avasthitam. He is present inside and outside of all the communities, of everything. So, this is the pastime here. Antarbahir Avasthitam. So, Kuntimani is able to perceive that Krishna's planetary expansion of Paramatma has entered the womb of Uttara, and protected the unborn son. Antarbahir Avasthitam. Bahir Nassim, Urdhir Nassim, which we chant every day. Then, why at all Krishna is not visible to the rebellious souls? Next shoka she says, Maya, Javanika, Channam. Because these conditioned souls become bewildered by Maya. Why? Agna, because Agnana, because of their ignorance towards self-realization. Adhokshaja. Akshaja means one which comes under purview of senses. We can perceive it. Adhokshaja means what? Which is beyond sense perception. He is beyond sense perception. Adhokshajam avyayam. What is it avyaya means? Very properly, very beautifully says, avyaya means which is, he is not touched by this material nature. He is free from that.

---
#### devotional service, Guru's mercy, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=1771
**Verse References:** BG 4.34
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So unless and until if we don't submit our free will and with all humility serve Guru, the import of Vedic literature will not be revealed."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a gold buyer without money to illustrate Krishna's detachment from unhappiness, and emphasizes the importance of association with pure devotees and submission to Guru for understanding Krishna's pastimes and Vedic literature.

To give you an example, if a person wants to buy some gold, and he goes to a gold shop, and a shopkeeper shows him everything, all the gold ornaments, different designs and cost, everything. Finally, to his surprise, the buyer sees that he has not brought his money. So he feels so unhappy, and even the shopkeeper feels so unhappy. Why I spend so much time with this man? So happiness is shared with him. So happiness is shared by both of them. So is it Krishna also feels unhappy when there is no one to do bhakti? Are you no one to do bhakti pa? I am unhappy. No. Eko devo nitya nilanurakto. Shastra says that Krishna, Supreme Personality of Godhead, is always engaged in his transcendental activities, transcendental pastimes along with his devotees, giving always happiness to them. They are all in ecstasy serving Krishna. So this is, what is that? What is that? Nalakshase mudad nalakshase mudadhrasa. So again, he is not visible to the foolish observer. Just like when Rama was there, Rama did not recognize. However, Krishna was there, Kamsa and many other demons did not recognize. Or when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was there, many Mahayavas did not recognize. When Srila Prabhupada was there, many of his godbrothers and many of now people are not recognizing his great position. So it is a lakshase mudadhrasa. They are so foolish observers. Because they don't come under the purview of this material nature. Nato nathya daro ratha. So he is saying that, if at all if you want to understand the pastimes and activities of Krishna, we can do only through the association of pure devotees. And here, ardently, through our own reception. This is a very important sloka from Bhagavad Gita. What is that? tad viddhi pranipatena pariprasnena sevaya upadekshyanti te jnanam So unless and until if we don't submit our free will and with all humility serve Guru, the import of Vedic literature will not be revealed. yasya deve para bhakti yatha deve tatha guruho yasya katha artha prakashyanti mahatma naha So automatically it will be revealed. The import of all Vedic literature will be automatically revealed to that person who has implicit faith on Guru and Krishna. That was the reason that's why Prabhupada had lot of faith on, implicit faith on his Guru. That's why he was able to give that, the pure Krishna consciousness to the people, to the whole world.

---
#### humility, absolute truth, Guru's mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Psw5mpLJDlQ?t=1853
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the great munis or amalatmas or parama hamsas are unable to understand you. We being a normal, common woman, what we can understand?"
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of aural reception of Guru's mercy and explains Kuntimani's humility in acknowledging that even great munis, amalatmas, and parama hamsas are unable to fully understand Krishna.

So, in many verses of scriptures also it says tasmad guru prapadyetajignasam senmuttamam sva-sotriyam brahmanishtam We are to receive the blessings or the mercy of Guru through the aural reception. Not Guru is appreciating you as such a nice devotee you are getting lot of money to me. Usually you are the best devotee, the best disciple of me. So till here, Kuntimani explains about nine philosophical points about nature of the absolute truth. And now, this is verse today, tata parama hamsa naam muni naam amalatma naam third verse tata parama hamsa naam muni naam amalatma naam Thus, she is saying that, actually this is in the height of humility this is what she is saying. Because in the first two verses she was able to perceive that Krishna through his Paramatma has entered the Uttara and protected him. And Krishna is Avyaya, Krishna is Ajokshada, Krishna is Ishvara, Krishna Sarvabhutanam, Alaksham Sarvabhutanam, Avastitamvairantarbhihi, all these philosophical points she is explaining. And out of great humility she is saying that, out of great humility she is explaining that, even the great munis or amalatmas or parama hamsas are unable to understand you. We being a normal, common woman, what we can understand?

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Chanchalapathi Dasa I SB 3. 30.15 I  03 11 2023
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### old age, detachment, miseries of material life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=97
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These are inescapable realities of life and so it's important that we reflect on it as spiritualists."
**Summary:** Lord Kapila's instructions to his mother focus on the miseries of old age, birth, and death, emphasizing the importance of reflecting on these realities as spiritualists.

Knowing that is considered to be a glorious death, but an attached family man wants his family members to carry him in a great procession even after his death. Although he will not be able to see how the procession goes, he still desires that his body be taken gorgeously in procession. Thus, he is happy without even knowing where he has to go when he leaves his body for the next life. So we are seeing in this chapter Lord Kapila's instructions to his mother, and particularly in great detail, we are hearing about the miseries of old age. Generally, we know birth, death, old age, and disease are the miseries, but here in this chapter of the Bhagavatam, in this section of the Bhagavatam, the miseries of old age are being more explicitly, more clearly described, so that we as human beings can reflect on this and can derive great lessons from this. Similarly, there are other parts of the Bhagavatam which describe the miseries of birth, and there are other sections which talk about the miseries of death. So these are inescapable realities of life and so it's important that we reflect on it as spiritualists. And in today's verse, Lord Kapila is saying...

---
#### attraction, detachment, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=716
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is that strong sense of attraction, I want to go back, I'll be going back to the association of that my family, my beloved, that actually drowns away the miseries."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a man returning to his family or a boy meeting his girlfriend to illustrate how strong attraction can overshadow miseries, suggesting that a similar attraction to Krishna can diminish the difficulties of life.

And it's a two-day journey, and when he is coming back, the two-day journey in the train, maybe he didn't get the right seat, dirty train, all dirty people around or whatever, didn't get good food. But Prabhupada explains the thought of the joy of being united with the family is so strong that he is just thinking when am I going to be with my family members, and he's so eager to be back with the family, and that joy fills those two days of his journey, and the miseries he goes through are actually not at all disturbing him. Recently, I was preaching to somebody and I gave this example to them, and then I gave a little twist to it to make it even more, you know, for people to understand a little bit strongly. I said imagine here is a boy, young boy, and after a long time he's coming back and he's going to meet his girlfriend or something whom he loves so much, and two days of journey he's absorbed in when I'm going to be with her, and so the difficulties that he goes through in the two days, he just doesn't bother at all. When I told this to this man, he's a respected man, about 50 year old man, he said yes, yes Swamiji, I can understand what you are saying about 30 years back and he started recalling, and I had to travel from Dehradun to Patna and there was no train and three days I had to go and then I was meeting and she pointed to his wife who was there sitting next to him. Everybody indicated that he had more questions about what you are saying about happening. I'm not a university student in Delhi. Days I had to go and then I was meeting and she pointed to his wife who was there sitting next to him and I was engaged to her, I was going to meet her, meet my fiance and with great difficulty I went this way, that way, bus and car train and unreserved, but I was so I didn't bother at all, I was going to meet like that. So actually this example of a man returning to the family or a young boy absorbed in the thought of meeting his girlfriend and wanting to be with her, this example that Sheila Prabhupada gives has one very important element in this, that is a sense of very strong attraction for the family or for the beloved. It is that strong sense of attraction, I want to go back, I'll be going back to the association of that my family, my beloved, that actually drowns away the miseries of the journey. The same way Krishna deity is teaching us that our lives more series will become facial makeup drowned and become insignificant provided we have developed that kind of a attraction for Krishna.

#### attraction, materialism, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=992
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When it comes to a spiritual or a religious program there's no attraction at all, he wants it to be as short as possible, one hour my god, make it as short as possible and the attraction is for some cricket match that's going on."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an anecdote about a man who wanted to shorten a religious ceremony to watch a cricket match, highlighting the contrast between attraction to material pursuits and spiritual activities.

Me one day and he said Swamiji, we are opening a new house and my wife is saying that we should call you to come and do a pooja. I said okay and his wife is, I could hear over here, his wife also speaking behind him and then Swamiji, my wife is asking how long the pooja will be. So I said maybe one hour, I said. Then he immediately said Swamiji, one hour, that's too long, can you make it short? I said okay, we can make it short, shall we make it half an hour? No, no, no Swamiji, even half an hour is too long. Can you make it 15 minutes? I said finally, okay 15 minutes. Soon after the call he said Swamiji, I'll talk to you later on, now there's India Pakistan cricket is going on match, I'll talk more details later on Swamiji and put the phone down. So see the contrast here, when it comes to a spiritual or a religious program there's no attraction at all, he wants it to be as short as possible, one hour my god, make it as short as possible and the attraction is for some cricket match that's going on and see that.

#### attraction, material world, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1080
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The most important principle of the material world is the attraction between man and woman and the most important principle or the dominant principle in the spiritual world is attraction between the living entity and Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada, stating that attraction is the driving force in both the material and spiritual worlds, with the former being the attraction between man and woman and the latter being the attraction between the living entity and Krishna.

So it's the attraction that drives a person. There is one great statement that Sheila Prabhupada makes in a letter to one devotee. Prabhupada says the most important principle of the material world is the attraction between man and woman and the most important principle or the dominant principle in the spiritual world is attraction between the living entity and Krishna. What is special about this statement of Sheila Prabhupada is actually what makes life interesting is that attraction. If there is no attraction life becomes dull. So Prabhupada's teaching Vedic literatures are offering there is a life of attraction in the spiritual world between the living entity and Krishna. The attraction between a man and a woman in the material world is contrived.

#### attraction, spiritual world, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual life is not just mechanical, repetitive, mindless rituals and you go on doing that. No. It is meant, it is required that is very essential to control the senses, to purify the senses, to purify the consciousness. But when we do that, by the mercy of Guru and Krishna, that attraction to Krishna is awakened."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that in the spiritual world, the attraction between living entities and Krishna is dynamic and constant, and that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's teachings reveal how Vedic processes purify the heart and awaken the soul's original attraction to Krishna, making spiritual life more than just mechanical rituals.

Whereas in the spiritual world the dominant force that is acting everywhere in the spiritual world is dynamic moment to moment attraction between the living entities and the Lord. The living entities want to serve the Lord and please him and the Lord wanting to serve the devotees and please them. Gopi jana vallabha giri varadhari Krishna is also thinking how I can please the gopis, how can I can please mother Yashoda. So the element of attraction which is actually the spice of life, which is actually what makes life interesting is not absent in the spiritual world. So in Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's teachings, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu points out that the Vedic processes that are mentioned are, it's called the sadhana, sadhana bhakti, vaidhi bhakti according to the regulative principles given in the Vedic literature, that when we do that we our heart gets purified and that original attraction the soul has for Krishna is awakened. Then the spiritual life looks completely different. This is a very very special hope and revelation of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu as it is there in the Vedic literature. So big control over spirituality, spiritual life is not just mechanical repetitive, not just mechanical, repetitive, mindless rituals and you go on doing that. No. It is meant, it is required that is very essential to control the senses, to purify the senses, to purify the consciousness. But when we do that, by the mercy of Guru and Krishna, that attraction to Krishna is awakened. That is the special thing of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, it is there in the Vedic literatures but even the previous Acharyas had not as clearly pointed out, revealed as Chaitanya Mahaprabhu reveals.

#### faith, Vaishnavas, Chaitanya Charitamrita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1338
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Faith, strong faith in the Vaishnava...our efforts are a qualification to receive, but you have to receive it. If it is not given, you cannot do anything about it. You have to wait and try to please a Vaishnava."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that faith in Vaishnavas is essential to awaken attraction to Krishna, and that while our efforts are a qualification, the attraction itself must be received as a gift from a Vaishnava. He then introduces the story of Kalidas, a devotee known for his immense faith in Vaishnavas.

And it is said Chaitanya Charitamruta reveals that there are certain things which are very beneficial to awaken that attraction and one important principle that is very essential to awaken that attraction is faith in the Vaishnavas because that has to be given by a Vaishnava. We cannot acquire it and achieve it by our efforts. Our efforts are a qualification to receive, but you have to receive it. If it is not given, you cannot do anything about it. You have to wait and try to please a Vaishnava. So faith, strong faith in the Vaishnava. To illustrate this, Chaitanya Charitamruta is a great devotee, his name was Kalidas. Actually he was an elderly devotee, he was the uncle of Raghunath Das Goswami and it is said that always he would be chanting Krishna's name even while doing ordinary work, doing different things, he would always be chanting Krishna's name. Another characteristic of this devotee Kalidas was he had great faith in the Vaishnavas and so what he would do, he would take all kinds of eatables, gifts and go and give it to a Vaishnava and then after the Vaishnava accepts it, he would induce him, please take it away from him, please eat it, honor it and he would make him after that with folded hands he would request him, can I get some remnants of the Prasadam of what you have eaten?

#### faith, Vaishnava Uchishta, Prasadam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Food offered to Krishna, it becomes Mahaprasadam and when that Mahaprasadam is eaten by a devotee, Vaishnava, pure devotee and the remnants of that are known as Maha Maha Prasadam."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Kalidas's devotion, explaining how he would seek Maha Maha Prasadam (remnants of food eaten by a pure devotee) and even retrieve discarded food to honor it, demonstrating his unwavering faith in Vaishnavas and the power of Vaishnava Uchishta.

Chaitanya Charitamruta describes food offered to Krishna, it becomes Mahaprasadam and when that Mahaprasadam is eaten by a devotee, Vaishnava, pure devotee and the remnants of that are known as Maha Maha Prasadam. So he would go to a devotee, give him all kinds of food and then request him, please give me Maha Maha Prasadam of yours. And sometimes devotees are generally very very humble, they would say no no no, we cannot give you, we cannot please, please thank you very much and would send him away. But he would go out and he would wait and hide. After some time when they come and throw the food, something from their house, he would immediately go there, take that one and lick that, so much faith in the Vaishnava and so much faith in the principle of Vaishnava Uchishta. This is called Vaishnava Uchishta, left remnants of what is left over by a pure devotee.

#### faith, Vaishnava Paada Dhooli, devotion

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He would go to a devotee's house, give them gifts and all that and then when he is, when they are sending him off, he would make the devotee walk a little distance and then he would send him back. While he is walking he would keep track of the steps and sometimes an impression is made and then after this person is gone, he would go there, take the dust from the footmarks and apply it on his head on his body."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Kalidas, describing how he would also collect the dust from the footprints of Vaishnavas (Vaishnava Paada Dhooli) and apply it to his body, further illustrating his profound faith and devotion.

So he would do that and then sometimes he would go to, another thing this Kalidas would do, he would go to a devotee's house, give them gifts and all that and then when he is, when they are sending him off, he would make the devotee walk a little distance and then he would send him back. While he is walking he would keep track of the steps and sometimes an impression is made and then after this person is gone, he would go there, take the dust from the footmarks and apply it on his head on his body. Vaishnava Paada Dhooli, Bhakta Paada Dhooli. So he had immense faith in this and he would always do that.

#### faith, Vaishnava, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1610
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami explains that Bhakta Paada Dhooli, Bhakta Paada Jala, Bhakta Bhukta Avashesha, Ina Mahabhala, these three things are considered to be very powerful to awaken that love of Krishna, that attraction to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts an incident where Kalidas drank the water that had washed Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's feet (Paada Jala), despite being forbidden, demonstrating his extreme faith. He then quotes Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami, emphasizing the power of Paada Dhooli, Paada Jala, and Bhakta Bhukta Avashesha to awaken love for Krishna.

And then he came to Jagannath Puri to see Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's daily practice was he would go to, there was one Narasimha Temple, he would go everyday to that Narasimha Temple and in that when he goes to Narasimha Temple, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu used to chant Namaste Narasimha Aya Prahlada Aladadai, the song we sing, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu used to sing that everyday. Lavakara Kamala Vare, all of that. So he would sing that and then go to Jagannath Temple and then there was a place where he would wash his feet and then walk up. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu had made a rule, no one should touch the water that has washed my feet and Govinda his assistant, Chaitanya used to have an assistant by name Govinda, he used to keep an eye on that one because it was Mahaprabhu's order. So one day when Kalidas came, he was waiting there. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Paada Jala was so special. So Govinda was pouring water, in a flick of a moment, he put his hand there and he drank it three times and then Chaitanya Mahaprabhu saw, enough, stop that, go away. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said thank you and then he went away, but he managed to sip three times Chaitanya Mahaprabhu Paada Jala. So Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami explains that Bhakta Paada Dhooli, Bhakta Paada Jala, Bhakta Bhukta Avashesha, Ina Mahabhala, these three things are considered to be very powerful to awaken that love of Krishna, that attraction to Krishna. Paada Dhooli, the dust from the lotus feet of a devotee, Paada Jala, water that has washed the lotus feet of a devotee and Bhakta Bhukta Avashesha, what? The remnants of the food that a devotee has eaten.

#### Prasadam, Srila Prabhupada, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1758
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada was very fond of Krishna Prasadam, always he would insist Prasabhadam is carried even if it's a short flight or a long flight, always devotees would carry Prasadam and Prabhupada would have at different times."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about Srila Prabhupada on a flight, emphasizing his fondness for Krishna Prasadam and how devotees always carried it for him. He describes how Prabhupada shared puffed rice Prasadam with his servant and secretary.

These are very very powerful. There is one very interesting pastime in Srila Prabhupada's life. Once Prabhupada was travelling from in a flight from Mexico City to Caracas, that's in South America, capital of Venezuela. So Prabhupada was travelling and Prabhupada was sitting at the window seat, next to him was his servant and next to him was another his secretary. His servant was Shrutakirti, next to him was Paramahamsa Swami, a sanyasi, his secretary. Actually whenever Prabhupada travelled they would pack some Prasadam and Prabhupada was very fond of Krishna Prasadam, always he would insist Prasabhadam is carried even if it's a short flight or a long flight, always devotees would carry Prasadam and Prabhupada would have at different times. So here in Mexico City temple they had made puffed rice, Muri puffed rice and fried with turmeric and seasoning and nice salt, that was one of the favourite dishes of Prabhupada. Prabhupada used to like that very much. So the devotees had made that very nicely and then after the flight took off, his servant pulled out from the bag, there was an aluminium foil, he opened it and kept it before Prabhupada and then Prabhupada ate a few times and then he gave it. So Prabhupada would eat little and then he would give it to the servant and then Shrutakirti accepted that, he divided that, made it into two and he gave one to Paramahamsa and he was eating. So very happy, Prabhupada's Mahamahaprasadam, they were happy.

#### Prasadam, mercy, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=1900
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "That's the good nature of a woman, always they want, they are always ready, eager to do service."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, describing how a stewardess on the flight unexpectedly ate some of the Prasadam and then offered to bring Prabhupada fruits and hot milk. Prabhupada appreciated her willingness to serve, highlighting the good nature of women.

Just then the steward in the aircraft, it was Venezuela Airlines. So a young woman walked by and she stood next to Paramahamsa and she was staring at what they are eating some very strange yellow puffed rice. For her it was and very strangely she put her hand into the little plate of Shrutakirti, took a little, put it in her mouth and looked at them and said very tasty and he was shocked and suddenly some hand coming and he was just recovering and then she said what is this? He didn't know, he said you know we are vegetarians and we eat some of these. Oh you are vegetarians, very nice, do you want something more? And so he asked Prabhupada, you want something, she is asking and so Prabhupada said fruits. Oh yes I will get you some fruits, she said and then again she asked, do you want anything to drink? And then he asked Prabhupada, she said Prabhupada, she is asking do you want to drink anything? And said Prabhupada said hot milk. Prabhupada liked hot milk also and so she said yes you can give hot milk and she said okay, if there is anything more also I will get for you and she went away. So Shrutakirti turned to Prabhupada and said Prabhupada, look at this, what a miracle, how she got, how she got maha maha prasadam. I mean he was trying to say that. Prabhupada said that's the good nature of a woman, always they want, they are always ready, eager to do service. Actually he was telling about maha maha prasadam, but then Prabhupada was looking at her willingness to bring some fruits and milk and all of those wanting to do. So Prabhupada appreciated her and then she went away and they brought the fruits, all of this happened then.

#### Prasadam, devotee, transformation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=2024
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "This is the potency of Maha Maha Prasadam of a Vaishnava, of a pure devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story by sharing how, 20 years later, a devotee revealed that the stewardess who had tasted the Prasadam on the flight had become a devotee herself, highlighting the transformative power of Maha Maha Prasadam.

Much much 20 years later Shrutakirti says he was describing this story in one Prabhupada festival in Los Angeles. So after the class one devotee walked up to him and said Prabhu I want to tell you one story and he said yes, what is this story? And then he described that how he was a South American devotee and how they would go from small town to town and they would do Harinam, they would do book distribution like that and would go house to house also and give some prasadam and books. So he said somewhere in Brazil, somewhere in a small town outside the town, this devotee and a few other devotees were going from house to house in a truck, in a jeep and showing books. So one house they knocked on and the door opened and there was a woman there and she saw oh Hare Krishna, very surprised in a place not expected and she said please come in, please come in and they walked into the house and they see in the house Prabhupada's picture, Krishna's picture, like a devotee's house. So they asked her how did you come in touch with Krishna consciousness and she said you know I met Srila Prabhupada. I said oh really how was that and then she said I was working as a steward in Venezuela Airlines and once I got an opportunity to serve Prabhupada and I ate Maha Prasadam also, Maha Maha Prasadam and that's how it started and then I somehow came in touch with devotees and then and so on and on and she has become a devotee. This is the potency of Maha Maha Prasadam of a Vaishnava, of a pure devotee.

#### faith, Vaishnava, attraction to Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5P5P3ACIaH4?t=2159
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If the miseries of our life should become drowned and become insignificant, it is very important that that strong feeling for Krishna, that is awakened in our heart."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating the importance of faith in Vaishnavas and the power of Krishna to awaken attraction to Him. He emphasizes that developing a strong feeling for Krishna can help overcome the miseries of life, referencing a verse about relishing transcendence.

So these are all very very special things about Krishna consciousness and Krishna Das Kaviraj Goswami says why it is said about these three Paada Jala Bhakta Paada Jala Bhakta Paada Dhooli Bhakta Bhukta Avashesha, it is because the background is it is that enormous faith, that faith in the Vaishnava. Vaishnava is related to Krishna and Krishna has given him the powers to give that attraction to him. If he gives that attraction is awakened. That is an important principle. So if our lives, if the miseries of our life should become drowned and become insignificant, it is very important that that strong feeling for Krishna that is awakened in our heart and we should be begging to Srila Prabhupada, begging to Krishna, begging to the holy name to awaken that. That is an important gift of Chaitanya Maha Prabhu. When that happens all these different kinds of miseries of old age, death, it will not bother us. Vimukto amrutam ashnute janmam rudyujara dukhair vimukto amrutam ashnute, one will be able to relish and taste transcendence. We will stop here. Grantharaj Srimad Bhagavatam ki, Shila Prabhupad ki.
