### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa I SB 2.2.23 I 09.11.2023
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### transcendentalism, materialism, spiritual body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The transcendentalists are concerned with the spiritual body."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of transcendentalists and their unrestricted movement compared to the limitations of materialists.

Yogi Veswaranam Gatimahurantah, Vahisthi Lokya Pavanantaratmanah, Na Karma Vistam Gatimapnubhanti, Vridyatapo Yoga Samadhi Bhajan. Translation and Purport by S. Jivayangres Abhaya Charanarvinda Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. The transcendentalists are concerned with the spiritual body. As such, by the strength of their devotional service, austerities, mystic power and transcendental knowledge, their movements are unrestricted within and beyond the material world. The fruity workers or the gross materialists can never move in such an unrestricted manner.

---
#### planetary travel, spiritual advancement, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=67
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By the strength of devotional service, austerities, mystic powers and transcendental knowledge, everyone can move like Narada Muni or Durvasa Muni."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts materialistic attempts at space travel with the transcendentalists' ability to move freely through spiritual advancement, citing Narada Muni and Durvasa Muni as examples.

The materialistic scientists endeavor to reach other planets by mechanical vehicle is only a futile attempt. One can, however, reach heavenly planets by virtuous activities. But one can never expect to go beyond swarga or janaloka by such mechanical or materialistic activities, either gross or subtle. The transcendentalists, who have nothing to do with the gross material body, can move anywhere within or beyond the material worlds. Within the material worlds, they move in the planetary system of mahar, janas, tapa and satraloka. And beyond the material worlds, they can move in the vaikuntha as unrestricted spacemen. Narada Muni is one of the example of such spacemen and Durvasa Muni is one such mystics. By the strength of devotional service, austerities, mystic powers and transcendental knowledge, everyone can move like Narada Muni or Durvasa Muni. It is said that Durvasa Muni traveled through the world the entirety of material space and part of spiritual space within one year only. The speed of the transcendentalists can never be attained by the gross or subtle materialists.

#### self-realization, body, planetary systems
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=157
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This body is not suitable for any other place other than this planet."
**Summary:** This segment explains the process of understanding the Lord and transferring one's soul to other planets, emphasizing the unsuitability of the current material body for other planetary systems.

So understanding the Lord in the heart, chapter of Bhagavatam, step by step, the process of knowing the Lord and transferring one's attention, transferring oneself to his original position is being explained here. So anybody who has the ability can transfer his body from this body, transfer his soul from this body to any other planet where he can get suitable body. As we saw the yogis, they can transfer themself. Wherever you want to go, we could go. You want to go to sun planet, want to go to moon planet. Scientists and astronomers, they want to go to many planets in this lifetime. That becomes difficult. You can go to the other planets but you will have to give up this body. Scientists, astronomers, they want to go in this body. Actually they are not even going in this body. They have to cover this body with so many other things. In this body they can't go. They have to cover with so many things which is conducive to the atmosphere in that planet. They cannot expose their body. Wear a coat and suit and I am going to moon. I am going to mars. Not possible. You have to wear the appropriate dress. If you wear the appropriate dress then you could go. So actually they don't, they don't realize that this body is not suitable for any other place other than this planet. Because they don't know. Because this body is made for this planet. This is earth. Earthly place. Earth, water, fire, all these things are present in this world. And our body also consists of these things.

#### body composition, elements, spiritual body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=297
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Different planets are having different kinds of elements in different proportion and that suitable body you can be there."
**Summary:** This segment details the composition of the body in relation to the elements of Earth and contrasts it with the elemental composition of other planets, suggesting the need for a suitable body to inhabit those planets.

Because pancha bhutas, maha pancha maha bhutas, earth, water, fire, air, ether, these things are the coverings of this material world. These are the substance with which this world is made. So with these things being in the outer space, it is also present in our body in small small proportion. So this planet which consists more of earth and water, our body is also more of earth and water. Majority of the body is water covered with fat, marrows and flesh and skin predominantly made of earth. Within this body there is this fire which helps us to sustain, create energy for movement, energy for digestion. Then this subtle energy, fire becomes, this fire becomes subtle energy in the form of all, all nervous systems, all electrical pulses around the body. As I say we have, body's heart is pumping six liters of this blood in the body. So we don't buy a half HP pump and fit it inside and pump it, connect it to your plug point. Where is the plug point? Where is the power? Body is generating all those things. So body generates power just like a generator. So this is all made for this planet. Suppose you are in sun planet, this body will burn. But say you have a fire body, will you burn? You will not burn. Some other planet, you have some other elements. So different planets are having different kinds of elements in different proportion and that suitable body you can be there. You go in this body and go to the, first of all you can go to sun. Say you are going to moon.

#### perception, spiritual realms, limitations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=437
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is foolish to go to these places because you are not supposed to be there."
**Summary:** This segment uses analogies of visiting the moon, the ghost world, and the ocean to illustrate the limitations of our current perception and the foolishness of trying to interact with realms for which we are not equipped.

They say they went to moon. Say you go to moon, now they want to go to mars. So they go to these places and look for, you know who is there, hello who is there. You can't see them nor can they hear you. But that doesn't mean there is nobody there, right? Say you go to the world of ghost and knock and say hello. The ghost can see you. They will say hello. But you can't see them. You can't hear them. So it is foolish to go to these places because you are not supposed to be there. Just like you can't go to the ocean and say hello to the dolphins and come. You need to have the appropriate clothing supporting your life. Then you can go there and play around. In the sky also you have something which keeps you floating. They can fly around and say hi to the birds, the geese are flying around. Same way you cannot go around the space and say oh Narada Muni is going there, Durvasa Muni is going there. Not possible. So we think these are all untrue. But if you sit back and think, why not? Right? If the birds can fly, fishes can swim and I can't do that. There may be other living entities who can do something better than me. Something which I can't do. And maybe they can't do what I can do here. The people in Mars cannot come and say see the earthling. No they cannot.

#### omnipresence, soul, subtle bodies
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=546
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Soul is everywhere."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the omnipresence of the soul and the existence of subtle bodies, emphasizing that just because we cannot perceive something with our senses does not mean it does not exist.

So even according to the material understanding, looking at these millions and millions of stars, it is foolish to think that we are the only people here. Because, because Keetha explains, spirit soul is sarvagataha, there is no place which is devoid of soul. Soul is everywhere. Even in this room, we are only soul 100 people or 50 people. No there are so many souls. There are so many ants here, much more than us. There are so many bacteria here. There is coronavirus also around. Maybe millions of them. So if you say what is the strength of this room? He will say 60. No no no. There are millions and millions of people here in this lecture. So you can say that, no no I was talking to millions of souls, not to 50s. So there are many things in this room which we cannot see. And there are subtle bodies. Maybe some ghost also here in this. Fortunate ghost. Every ghost cannot hear Bhagavatam. Some fortunate ghost can hear. So living entities in subtle form are existing. So just because we cannot see, we cannot perceive with the senses doesn't mean they don't exist. That is the first understanding of a person who knows transcendental knowledge.

#### transcendental knowledge, yoga, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=622
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as we provide these things that the body wants, we cannot be transcendentalist."
**Summary:** This segment explains how transcendental knowledge allows us to see the truth in scriptures, and how yogis strive to free themselves from bodily and mental restrictions through sense control.

With that understanding we can see truth in what Bhagavatam and Shastras say. Shastras never tell untrue because they know more than us. So we are restricted. So the yogis try to free themselves from this restricted body and restricted mind. Because they know the soul is much much more powerful than this body and this mind, this subtle body. So they want to go away. Go above this material form. How? Through the process of yoga. The process of yoga involves sense control. They are all called transcendentalists. Whether you are a yogi or you are a jnani, whether a monist or personalist or a Vaishnava. They are all called transcendentalists. Transcendentalist generally means anybody who wants to transcend this material world. Whoever wants to go above the material world. So if you want to go above the material world then you have to deny or you have to be free from the limitations of this world. Then only you can be a transcendentalist. As long as you are drowning in the water you cannot say that you are out of water. When you come out of water then you are out of water. So you need to deny the rules of water. Then you can get into the rules of earth, the shore. So as long as we don't deny the activities of the material world. What are the activities of the material world? Sensitification. Providing what the eyes want. Providing what the nose wants. Providing what the mouth wants. Tongue wants. The skin wants. So as long as we provide these things that the body wants, we cannot be transcendentalist.

#### transcendentalism, subtle energies, material desires
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=736
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as you want to free yourself from this material, the requirements of the body then you are a transcendentalist."
**Summary:** This segment further defines transcendentalism, emphasizing freedom from both gross and subtle material energies, and introduces the concept of 'trying transcendentalists' who are on the path of bhakti.

Some people become out of body but they keep giving the materials required for the mind, for the intelligence, for the ego. They are also not transcendentalists because they are stuck with the subtle energies of this material world. Not only the gross energies of the material world but the subtle energies. So they are also called embodied. They are also called muk. They are not the bhadha jivas. But as long as you want to free yourself from this material, the requirements of the body then you are a transcendentalist. Even when you are trying, you are called a trying transcendentalist. So we are also transcendentalists. Trying transcendentalists. We are not following the yoga process or the jnana process. We are following bhakti. Bhakti process. Whichever the process is, you are called transcendentalist. So these transcendentalists try to free themselves from these bodily limitations and rise above the body. Above this world, they go beyond this world, Svargaloka. Prabhupada says in this parbhud they cannot go beyond Svarga. Bhuvah, Svaha, Maha, Jnana, Tapa, Satya. These are the seven planetary systems above. Similarly seven below. So fourteen planetary systems. So up to Svarga you can go with material desires.

#### spiritual knowledge, bodily bondages, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=826
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as we deny the requirements of the body, we are transcendentalist."
**Summary:** This segment explains that souls in higher planetary systems are more advanced in spiritual knowledge and free from bodily bondages, and that liberation is possible even on Earth by denying bodily requirements.

Prabhupada says Jnana, which is part of Svarga. If you want to go to Maha, Tapa, Satya, you can go with the material desires. Prabhupada says Jnana, which is part of Svarga. If you want to go to Maha, Tapa, Satya, you cannot. So the people who are the souls who are in those planets are much much more advanced. Advanced in spiritual knowledge. Means they are free from the bodily bondages. They don't feel hungry like us. They don't feel the senses are just like us. But they are also in the material world. So half of the people in the material world are controllers of the body. But in this earth, you have two options. You can either be free from the bodily conditions or you can be within the bodily conditions. So you can be liberated in this material world, in this earth also. So as soon as we deny the requirements of the body, we are transcendentalist. And that is a prerequisite to go beyond this body and mind. Deny the requirements. So in devotional service or in yoga or any spiritual process, bodily requirements are limited. So there is rules and regulations. Do this, don't do this. Regulatory principles. Follow these principles. Do this sadhana. These are all processes of elevating the soul out of this body.

Just like in this world also, you want to go to the richest country. I want to be in the richest country in this world. Which is the richest country? Now? USA is gone. No more. Say some, I don't know, Denmark, Finland, they are rich. They won't allow you, unless you are also rich like them. First you have to show a balance sheet, your income tax, how much money you have, how much wealth you have, how good you are, what is your professional capacity, what are your abilities, can you contribute to the country, and then you come. So even in this material world, you cannot go to rich places. You want to go to Sudan? No problem. Because you are qualified to go there. Because people there are less qualified than you. But if you want to go to a place which is more qualified than you, conditions are there.

So even in this world there are conditions. So if you have good karma, good money, good intelligence, then you are welcome to good places. Same way, if you have good karma, if you have controlled your body, you can go to a place better than this place. Svarga, you can enjoy so many things. Beyond Svarga, Chana, Tapa, Maharloka, Satyaloka, all are yogis. They can go anywhere. Just take off. Travel anywhere. They might be sitting and wondering what these people in earth are doing. Making aeroplanes, making rockets, and getting blasted. Laughter. Why are they struggling so much? The same energy, the same power, the same tapasya. Because all this needs tapasya, right? So much money is needed, so much practice is needed, so much technology is needed for putting one thing to work. So if the same time is involved in internal development, then you can levitate. Right? Why you need food? Aircrafts. So the soul can be lifted up. The problem is that I want to go with my material desire. I want to go with this mind. I want to go with this conditioning. That is not possible. So we are controlled. So transcendentalists want to go beyond this material conditioning. So the yogic transcendentalists, they can go to Satyaloka, Tapaloka, beyond Svarga. Brahma is in Satyaloka. Everything is dissolved in this material world. Brahmaloka will always be there. Satyaloka will always be there. Until the day of Brahma. That also has to wind up. So traveling to different planets in this material world is not a very big exciting thing for a transcendentalist who want to go beyond this material world.

#### bhakti yoga, science of Krishna consciousness, transcendental body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=1122

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This science of Krishna consciousness has to be understood."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that bhakti yogis understand the Lord beyond the material world as their destination, and that Krishna consciousness is a science of understanding how to attain a transcendental body and escape the prison house of the material world.

So that is easy for bhakti yogis because bhakti yogis, they understand the Lord who is in this beyond this material world, that is my destination. This is not my destination. My destination is there with the Lord. The Lord with the transcendental body and I also have a transcendental body which is engaged today. That is why this world is called Durga, prison house. We are in the prison house, limited by this body, by the iron bars of this body. So when we are liberated, we are in this spiritual world. So this is called science of Krishna consciousness. Prabhupada always mentioned. This science of Krishna consciousness has to be understood. Those who understand this science, Prabhupada does not say religion, this belief, this faith. This is beyond belief and faith and religion. This is pure science. So people say, no, no, no I cannot be sectarian, I have to be secular. We are not even saying secular or sectarian, we are saying scientific. We are all scientists. We are all scientists. Chandras, Tilak, Shikha. What are we science? What is the science we are following? The science of getting out of this material body. The science of going beyond this body which is limiting us and going to a world which is unlimited. So that world which is unlimited, like Brahma Samhita says, The world, the real world of spiritual life. No material body. So to understand that there is personalism beyond this material limitation, you need Sumedha Saha.

#### intelligence, shastra, liberation

**Summary:** This segment explains that intelligence is needed to understand personalism beyond material limitations, and that this intelligence comes from reading the right Shastra, leading to the process of awakening the soul through chanting the Hare Krishna Mahamantra.

You need intelligence. And that intelligence comes by reading the right Shastra. Not people who want to create something of their own. That is the beauty of Bhagavatam in the Vaishnava Parampara which clearly describes our rotation and clearly describes what destination we have to go to and how to go to that destination. That is Bhagavatam philosophy. Step by step going out. It may take many lifetimes. Devotees are not concerned about that. Devotees are concerned that am I gradually understanding the supreme truth in its real form and am I understanding the cheating which is going on in this body. Cheating means limitations which is going on in the body. Which is controlling my soul within this body. By making me do what the body wants. By making me do what my mind wants. And what my false ego wants. And not allowing me to do what my soul wants. Soul is sleeping. So the process of awakening the soul is this great chanting of liberation like Prabhupada says. This chanting of Hare Krishna Mahamantra is a great chanting for liberation.

#### liberation, yoga, devotional service

**Summary:** This segment contrasts liberation through sound (chanting) with yogic practices, explaining how yogis control life air to pull out the soul, while transcendentalists dissolve themselves through negation, and devotees understand the activity of the soul to gain admission into the spiritual world.

Because sound, through sound we can liberate. Yogis try to liberate through yogic practices. Because the life air is controlled. The soul is balanced by the life force within the body. So by controlling the life air he can pull out the soul out. Like we saw in previous verses. How a yogi can pull out from the cerebrum of the head. They can pull out. Transcendentalist can dissolve himself. Naithi, naithi, naithi, I am not this body, I am not this body. I am not this matter, I am not this mind. And he understands spirit means some impersonal energy which is also part of the absolute. Fortunate yogis can go up to Paramatma. And devotees they understand the activity of the soul. When you understand the activity of the soul then we get admission into the spiritual world. Where we can continue activity free from all the limitations. Activities are not stopped. So we in the material world sometimes we don't understand what is spiritual activity. Because we know activity means limitation. I can do certain thing. I can only lift 100 kilos. Or maybe 50 kilos. I am limited by my body. I can understand some formula. I can understand beyond that. I am limited by my intelligence. I can desire and own and control this many number of people of this caliber. Not beyond that. Again I am controlled. So that is what happens in this material world of control. How do we go beyond this control? But that doesn't mean that I am not at all controlled. Spiritually also we are controlled.

#### spiritual control, service, limitations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=1424
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spirit souls are supposed to serve the Supreme Whole. Supreme Lord Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains that spiritual control is different from material control, as it is a mutually useful relationship like the fingers and the body, where the spirit soul serves the Supreme Lord and is also served in return, free from material limitations.

We are eternally controlled. But we are controlled by the Supreme Lord. His internal energy. Control means not like material control. It is just like saying this small finger is controlled by the body. It is not that this is restricted. That control has no restriction. This control has utilization. This small pinky finger is used for this body. What the body wants it does. That way it is subservient to the body. But it is part of the body. And it is also enjoying along with the body. Same way when Prabhupada says the spirit soul is part and parcel of the whole. And it is supposed to serve the whole. It does not mean the servant relationship in the material world. It is a mutually useful relation. Just like the fingers and the body. So the spirit souls are supposed to serve the Supreme Whole. Supreme Lord Krishna. And by that service he is also served. He is also happy. He is also nourished. That is a connection of a devotee and Supreme Lord. Which is full of activities. Which is free from material limitations. Starting from false ego. And all the limiting factors in the material world. Which is different things. Karma, Krodha, Moha, Mada, Matsarya. All these things limit us. Anger limits us.

#### addiction, spiritual habits, Krishna's energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=1516
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is up to us whether we want to run around this universe in bliss or we have to run around with fear."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how karma makes us dependent on material things, and how we can simultaneously practice addiction to spiritual things like chanting, associating with the transcendental, and surrendering to Krishna's spiritual energy, contrasting Narada's blissful travels with Durvasa's fearful running.

Karma limits us. It doesn't limit us. It makes us a person. Makes us a person who is dependent. Addicted to that. Addicted to material things. But simultaneously we practice being addicted to spiritual things. Gradually, gradually. Chanting of the holy name. Which is transcendental. We are associating with transcendental. So we want to be controlled by the spiritual energy of Krishna. Materialists want to be controlled by the material energy of Krishna. Like two personalities given example here. Narada and Durvasa. Narada is controlled by Krishna. But he is free. He can travel anywhere. You can say Durvasa also travelled everywhere. But he is not under the control of Krishna. See what happened to be under the control of Maya. He was a great mystic. But he had this ambition to be a great person. He had the arrogance of belittling others. As long as he has all this arrogance belittling others. Pride. Karma. You are controlling the material world. So that is a controlled life. He is running around the universe. Why is he running around? He is running around because Sudarshan is behind him. But he has a capacity to run because he was a great yogi. He is going to Shivaloka, Brahmaloka almost like Vaikuntha. Must be Swetha Deepa. See that Swetha Deepa is a spiritual world. Even though it is part of material world. He is running everywhere. Narada is also running everywhere. But he is running in bliss. So it is up to us whether we want to run around this universe in bliss or we have to run around with fear. As long as we are in the grip of material world we are running around with fear.

#### Krishna's energy, consciousness, material contamination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=1628
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The energy of the Lord can be displayed according to our consciousness."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of a cat carrying a kitten versus a rat to illustrate how Krishna's energy manifests differently based on our consciousness, and emphasizes the importance of freeing ourselves from material contamination to orbit in a spiritual consciousness.

As it is said Krishna's energy of two types. One is energy which is supporting you. Other one is energy which is punishing you. Prabhupada is an example of a cat. The same cat. Same teeth. Very very sharp teeth. It is carrying a kitten. The same teeth it is carrying a rat. A rat is like Durvasa. Shivering and afraid in the mouth of the cat. Narada is like a kitten. In the same mouth. So the energy of the Lord can be displayed according to our consciousness. If our consciousness is materially contaminated then we are in a different consciousness. Material consciousness. Which is limiting. If we are in a spiritual consciousness surrendered to the Supreme Lord then we are orbiting in a different orbit. So it is up to us. Today Bhagavatam step by step is teaching how to go beyond the material thing to the transcendental world. So transcendentalists are not interested in roaming around in this world. But they are more interested in freeing themselves and seeing their real form in the spirit, as a spirit soul. And engage in Krishna's service in the spiritual world. So that should be the goal of our life. Which may mean denying certain things in this material world. Without sacrificing. We don't get that. So we need to sacrifice material things. So material things are name, fame position power, money women, family business, power to control. When we give these things up

#### spiritual habits, transcendentalists, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m6qtPxtFAh0?t=1740
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To give up to become surrendered is very very difficult. Most difficult thing."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts material and spiritual habits, defining spiritual habits as surrender, humility, knowledge, and friendliness, and distinguishes between devotees dedicated to the Lord and frustrated impersonalists, emphasizing that surrender is the most difficult thing to achieve.

Then we inculcate spiritual habits. Spiritual habits are surrender, humility knowledge always differentiating what is matter, what is spirit. Engaging only in spiritual activity. Always subservient to the Supreme Lord. And always friendly to all the other creations of the Lord. Not only that we are subservient to the Lord we are subservient to all the other things. All the living entity is created by the Lord. Then he becomes a friend of all the living entity. Then he becomes friend of this world also. Water, air forest soil, energies everything is Lord's. So he uses it all in a friendly way. Not in an exploiting way. So two kinds of transcendentalists. One kind of transcendentalists who are called devotees. They are dedicated to the Lord. Other kind of transcendentalists are people who are frustrated with the material world. And they are in a denying position. They are still trying to exploit the Lord. Even impersonalists are not dedicated. They are not surrendered. They are out of the material world but they are not surrendered. So devotee is the only person who is surrendered and who is in the real reality. Others are all assumed reality. Material world is assumed reality. Impersonalism is also a kind of assumed reality because he is not coming to his real position of subservient. He also wants to be like God or Brahma Jyoti. There is still no surrender there. To give up to become surrendered is very very difficult. Most difficult thing.

You may say yoga is very difficult. Jnana is very difficult. But actually surrendering is most difficult. So bhakti yuga they say bhakti yuga is for all japa

The nuclear weapon generates energy by converting matter into energy by atomic fission or fusion, and the mass is transformed into energy. I think two weeks back I had taken class, all of you were here, explained how the Brahmastra, if we had read about it in the year 1900 beginning, no scientist will believe that, oh, what is this they are talking Brahmastra, a weapon which is like a, just like that, from nothing, light would come out, heat would come out, they would never believe. But after 1920s, later on, after Albert Einstein wrote that famous equation, E is equal to mc squared, that mass can be converted into energy, so mass is everywhere, atoms are everywhere, any atom can be converted into energy. It is a very difficult process, means difficult in modern technology, but a well-trained Brahmana could release this Brahmastra, could release this energy, and this is what we discussed last time, how whatever the Shastra says, it is a great science, science is included, this modern science is included in the Bhagavata.

He emphasizes the importance of focused Japa, free from distractions caused by the mind's various activities.

Therefore the existence, existence which is, existence means eternal existence, eternal existence is trapped in this body of non-existence. Beyond this body, eternal existence is beyond this body. This body is beginning to end, so one small existence we experience in this body, but actually we the soul is eternal existence. So we are actually trapped in this non-existence, non-existence means anything that has beginning and end has no existence ultimately. Existence means it should forever remain there. So we are trapped, the real existence or real Sat is trapped in Asat body. So by touching the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, by touching the sound Hare Krishna Mahamantra, when there is flow of Sat energy by the mercy of the Lord, then one existence would lighten up, its eternal existence beyond this body, but it is energy that has to come into you, that energy can come only by associating with Hare Krishna Mahamantra. Then we discussed how actually we can come in touch with the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, the real way touch the Hare Krishna Mahamantra is through Japa, but then Japa should be done in a right way so that the touching of the soul and the Hare Krishna sound happens. For that we said we have to be attentive to hear the holy name during Japa, and then we discussed how the mind creates, takes away attention from Japa into various different thinking processes, processing, lamenting, rejoicing, dreaming, planning, all this kind of things activity our mind gets sucked into it while we are doing the Japa. So when the attention is sucked by all these activities, thinking activities, attention is not available for hearing the chanting that you are doing. #### Japa, Attention, Mind, Offenses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMJcRxc2MK0?t=477
**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of attentive hearing during Japa, warning against mechanical chanting where the mind wanders. He uses the analogy of iron in the fire to illustrate the need for continuous attention to spiritualize the Atma, and highlights inattentiveness as one of the ten offenses during chanting.

Then after that, your Japa is just automatic. You are chanting round after round, and then your Japa is finishing but your thinking has not finished. You are continuing your problem solving, planning, lamenting, rejoicing, dreaming, worrying, or doing some other activity. I did, last time we discussed, you are doing some other activity, your mind is busy directing your senses but you are automatically chanting. The whole key thing is the sound you have to hear. We discussed the example of the iron put in the fire, and the iron is put in the fire slowly, it gets heated heated heated and becomes red hot. So in the same way, the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, if you touch the sound, then our Atma will get spiritualized, the spiritual energy will come from the holy name, how we will get spiritualized. But it is not in a, suppose you take a piece of iron, put in the fire, again take it out, five minutes, then again one minute in the fire, then again five minutes outside, then again one minute inside, five minutes outside, will it get really heated? No because you put inside, it comes out again it is cooling off, isn't it? So in the same way, if we chant Hare Krishna Mahamantra, if you give attention to it, we have to give continuous attention to finish a Mala. In between you should not do anything. If you do anything then you are taking out of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, you are taking your soul out, gets disconnected. And why disconnection also is like a inattentive means one of the ten offences is what? To be inattentive while chanting. So you are attentive little, taking attention away, some thinking, again thinking, or sometimes nothing, not even hearing one. So what is required is you should make a sankalpa to keep the iron in the fire for quite some time, at least one Mala you take, nothing I shall do now from beginning to end of this Mala, I shall continue to do this only nothing else. And we discussed how mind can do one activity which will not disturb Japa, what is that activity can somebody tell who was there? Checking, following and checking, so following and checking continuously, you should not break, why you should not break? Because if you break, mind doesn't keep quiet, it goes to some other subject, that means you get disconnected from the Shabda. So you have to follow, follow and check.

For instance if he does not release the sankalpa, I have started now, first bead, he should release the sankalpa from the soul, sankalpa shakti comes from the soul, from the sankalpa, then it goes to the mind, then Krishna deepens will help you make that sankalpa stronger, but you have to start your sankalpa, I want to hear without distraction, that sankalpa is there, then you will see that it will happen. If you don't have sankalpa, simply Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Ramaking, Hare Hare, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, if you don't have sankalpa shakti from the soul coming you will see suddenly somewhere mind will take and go away, and then you chanting will become mechanical, your consciousness attention is somewhere else. So each of our soul shakti, ICCHA shakti, ICCHA is very clear to touch the Holy name of the Lord, then sankalpa should be there to finish mala after mala after mala after mala. What is meditation of seeing? Don't take your vision from something. Be there only. Be concentrated, concentrated, concentrated seeing. Say, I keep on seeing this mic. Don't take your mind, don't take your mind anywhere other than that. Seeing, seeing, seeing, seeing. Japa is not seeing meditation. Japa is doing meditation. That means don't do anything else. Stay only this, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing. Complete, mala, beat after beat, beat after beat, beat after beat. That is, that is why you need, you don't need seeing concentration for japa. You need doing concentration. There are two things that are happening. One doing is you are speaking. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, speaking. Another doing is what? You are hearing. There are two activities going on. Speaking and hearing, speaking and hearing, speaking and hearing. This activity alone, you follow this. You follow your own speaking and you follow your own hearing. Then you will see that. Automatically, the mind is fully engaged. The mind will not go somewhere else. And I explained last time also, the spiritual technology, why the, our acharyas are given mala to do japa with mala. It is to give work to the mind. To give work to the mind. Otherwise, mind will do unrelated work. Let it at least do a work which is connected to japa. Okay, I heard. One is over. Two is over. Three is over. It's connected work. When you chant. Initially, you start with the first beat. I have to hear. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Then next, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama. Between the two beats, the sankalpa shakti should be there to go, to go forward. Should not. ### Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 1.7.27 | 17-01-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### detachment, knowledge, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6wlP6Mb2Dvw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who use this knowledge and separate themselves by knowledge, they separate themselves from the body."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the story of Ashwathama releasing the Brahmastra and how knowledge leads to detachment from the material body and liberation, enabling one to dedicate their life to sharing this knowledge.

So this is a chapter titled 'The Son of Drona Punished,' who is Ashwathama. All of a sudden, there was a great effulgence, dangerous effulgence, destructive effulgence spreading all around. Krishna and Arjuna were discussing, where does it come from? Arjuna was saying, where does it come from? I do not understand. So the Supreme Lord replies that this is Brahmastra released by the son of Drona, and he does not know how to retract the glare. That is coming from this. So that knowledge does not necessarily mean that you have to physically separate from this body, but those who use this knowledge and separate themselves by knowledge, they separate themselves from the body. They feel so much liberated that they utilize the rest of their life in this body to give this knowledge to others and make them also feel this liberating feeling of having come out of this misidentification with this body. So one who utilizes this life to chant these mantras, Hare Krishna, names of God, to do japa, to do kirtana, have faith in the holy name. Once he gets this detachment from this material body.

#### body consciousness, spiritual purpose, scriptural guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6wlP6Mb2Dvw?t=115
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who think they are this body, their soul is living for the body."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the lives of those who identify with the body, whose souls serve the body's desires, with those who understand their spiritual purpose and use their bodies according to scriptural principles to transcend the material world.

Then he does not live for the body, but he makes the body live for him. Just like those who think they are this body, their soul is living for the body. What I will do with the body, how I will secure my body, how I will give gratification for my body, how I will feed my senses. These are all the chinta of a person who is thinking, 'I am the body.' And the thoughts of a person who is thinking, 'I am not the body' is that I, as a spirit soul, I have a different purpose. I have understood from the scriptures that the purpose of getting this human life is to come out of this material world. And therefore the shastras are given certain techniques, certain methods. I should give this material life. I should give this material life. I should give this material life. I should use this material life. I should be able to utilize that body according to those principles and come out of this body.

So first, you get little knowledge, jnana, that I am not this body. And then you get convinced that you should adopt spiritual practices given by Srila Prabhupada. And then after you do those spiritual practices, slowly the knowledge becomes deeper and it becomes vijnana, or realized knowledge. I am not this material life. I am not this material body. So initially we may not fully get that jnana shakti that I am not this body, but you have faith in Srila Prabhupada's faith in guru, sadhu, and shastra, and then therefore you simply follow the instructions given by them. Then you will see that that jnana will become vijnana. I am not this body. Your existence beyond this dead material body, you will start experiencing it. And you experience that, then your spiritual practices will get reinforced. Yes, my body should be controlled. My body should be run or managed in a manner in which it is going to help me realize my purpose of the journey in this human body, my real atma's purpose. I should start doing that.

#### japa, intention, attention, spiritual connection

**Summary:** The speaker explains that during japa, intention and faith are crucial for maintaining attention on the holy name, allowing the sound to penetrate the soul, emphasizing that the Hare Krishna mantra contains all potencies and the degree of benefit depends on how well one connects with it.

Then your attention will remain on the holy name. The same way when you do japa, you should have intention. The intention should be clear why you are doing japa. Just like intention is clear why you are your attention is on what I am saying because you want to get knowledge, you want to get spiritual knowledge. Now the shastra says that Hare Krishna maha mantra is a source of all knowledge, is the source of all ananda. Now I should take that inside me. The process of japa as I said the spiritual technology is that this sound should go inside and touch the atma. If your attention is not there, it will not go inside. If your attention is there, it will come inside you. Consider your attention like your spiritual hand coming out of this body. Only one thing can come out of this body though you're trapped. One thing can come out of this can come out, one spiritual thing can come out. What is that? Attention. So, if you have attention on the sound, then you are actually touching the sound which is coming here and immediately there is connection between soul and the sound. So, the Hare Krishna, Krishna Kalikale Nama Rupa Avatara, Sarva Shakti, all Shakti is included in this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. How much, it all depends upon you, how much you are, how well you are touching this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra that you chant.

Material love is appearing to be like spiritual love, but no. It is asat, achit, nirananda. But what we are looking for is? Sat, chit, ananda. In material world, material activities, you will only get asat, achit and nirananda. Second, the mind also does planning. I have some objective. Ok, I will do this, I will do this, no, no, I will not do this, I will do this, I will take this route, this route, planning. Agreed? Yes. The soul is doing and the mind is following, the soul is chanting and hearing and your mind is following chanting and hearing. This is not some new technology that I am saying now. Chanting is very simple. Only when the attention goes away in other activities which I mentioned, then you won't hear. Now, if you have to count, you have to hear. Is it not? If you have to move the next bead, you have to hear. Without making it automatic, you have to consciously, you have to engage your mind in following your japa. Now, how to do that in following the japa? One more thing is there. Real effective method. After following, one more activity the mind can do. That is called checking. Inspector job. Mind should be given inspector job. What is the inspector job? Have I chanted the full mantra properly? Anyway, Hare Krishna missed out? Don't believe your mind. Don't believe. You simply you say that you check whether every mantra is chanted properly. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. The mantra has got two parts. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Hare Krishna string and Hare Rama string. ### Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.20.37 | 23-02-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### ignorance, illusion, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=59
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only those who are captivated by the mode of ignorance and passion will subject themselves to this force of false attraction, illusory attraction in this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Asuras are captivated by ignorance and are thus attracted to the illusory attractions of the material world, unlike intelligent people who understand things as they are.

The Asuras were completely floored by the beautiful woman, and after that, they simply seized her, grabbed her, embraced her. Prabhupada is explaining here that the Asuras are described as Mudadhya, meaning they are captivated by ignorance. So only those who are captivated by the mode of ignorance and passion will subject themselves to this force of false attraction, illusory attraction in this material world, and they are considered to be Mudadhya, means not very intelligent or they are foolish. One who knows things as they are, then one would not run after the things in this material world.

---
#### illusion, reality, perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For the senses, it looks like gold. There's no difference between the rolled gold and the plated gold and real gold. There's no difference for the senses, but for the value, there'll be a difference."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of real gold versus plated gold, the speaker explains that while both may appear the same to the senses, only real gold has true value and existential truth.

In other words, if you really want to buy gold, then you have to... unless you get real gold, you are cheated. Something will be gold-like, plated gold. It will look like gold for the senses. There's no difference between the rolled gold and the plated gold and real gold. There's no difference for the senses, but for the value, there'll be a difference. One has no value, the other has got very high value comparatively, and one has no value. So one gives experience of gold, the other one is real gold, and real gold also gives experience of gold, but it has also got the value of gold. That is a difference. One is experientially true, the other one is experientially true and existentially true. In existence also, it is real gold.

---
#### truth, experience, spiritual knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=238
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To establish the existential truth of something, any amount of experiences with the senses will not determine that truth. One has to go beyond the senses and go to the intelligence."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that true knowledge requires going beyond sensory experience and using intelligence, guided by spiritual knowledge from Guru, Sadhu, and Shastra, to determine existential truth.

So any sense perception, anything that we see, if it is only true experientially, it is not necessary that it is truth. Truth has to be established independently on the basis of experience. For instance, you watch a movie. Once you go into the movie world, it's a real experience. The experience is as real as real life experience, no difference. But is that true existentially? No, experientially true, but existentially false. Now, if you want to analyze, you want to whether that movie is existentially true or experientially... I am experientially seeing it is true. Now I want to find out whether it is existentially true. So you analyze the movie more and more. The movie plot, you go into the movie plot. Okay, in the movie plot, this character, that character, you go on analyzing. Will you ever come out of the movie? No. In other words, to establish the existential truth of something, any amount of experiences with the senses will not determine that truth. One has to go beyond the senses and go to the intelligence, and intelligence, the source of that intelligence should be independent of that movie, because intelligence is not coming from the movie. And that is the third source. It is not coming from the movie, and that is spiritual knowledge: Guru, Sadhu, and Shastra.

---
#### spiritual knowledge, illusion, senses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=398
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is only way to establish whether an experience is existentially true or not, is by a third source, that is Shastra."
**Summary:** Spiritual knowledge, though available materially, represents transcendental knowledge independent of sensual perceptions, which are prone to illusion. The only way to establish existential truth is through Shastra.

The spiritual knowledge, even though it is available in ordinary, material paper, material ink, it is available here, present truth, but that is not real spiritual knowledge. It is manifesting like this, like any other ordinary book, but the vibration, the sound that is generated, the sound that it represents, this transcendental knowledge, that is the independent source, independent of our sensual perceptions. Our sensual perceptions can make mistakes, commit tendencies for illusion, cheating, all these things. So there is no question of establishing whether something is illusion or whether something is true by your senses, you cannot. There is only way to establish whether an experience is existentially true or not, is by a third source, that is Shastra.

#### ignorance, intelligence, experience

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The person is influenced by only experience, not using the intelligence."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the foolish Asuras who rely solely on sensory experience with those who use intelligence to discern truth, using the example of buying gold-plated items instead of real gold.

So the Asuras, muddhya means foolish, they go only by experience, mode of passion. Right now, yes, this experience is real, so it must be real. That is enough for them. But for somebody who is having that weakness, suppose somebody is only experience, he only wants experience of gold. He goes to a shop full of gold-plated items, cheating, and the person wants, oh, so nice, like gold, gold, shining, oh, this is nice shining gold, give me that. He will pay the price of gold and take it. The person is influenced by only experience, not using the intelligence. Okay, what is the weight of this material? What is the volume of this material? What is the density of this material? Oh, it cannot be gold. It is so light. Gold is very heavy, 19.5 grams per cc. This is like copper, and all these things are only 8 grams, 9 grams. Same volume will weigh less. You take something by weight itself you will say, oh this necklace is not original necklace, it's only plat. You ask, why? Why does it not get, it is not seeing that. He is using intelligence, knowledge and intelligence.

#### pleasure, illusion, devotion

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every experience in this material world, every sense experience is illusion. Among the illusions, again there is filter, what is permitted by the shastras."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that all sense experiences in the material world are illusions, but some are permitted by the shastras. Devotees fall into the category of authorized sense gratification, which is better than being an Asura.

Pleasure means, experience means, I'll jump over it. Whereas a gentleman, a human being, who is progressive in his life, he accepts pleasures which are only, not only experiential, but it should also have, and every experience in this material world, every sense experience is illusion. Among the illusions, again there is filter, what is permitted by the shastras. Just like Krishna says, I am sex life according to regulative principles. So, a sura, or a devotee, he falls in love. He falls into the category of authorized sense gratification. That's kamabhakta. They are not talking about pure devotees. Asuras and suras, you can say. Whereas a pure devotee, he sees that this is all illusion. Authorized or unauthorized, it's all illusion. If I can come out of it, that is the purpose of moving from asuric thing to suric thing. That means by becoming an obedient sense enjoyer is better than being an Asura.

#### illusion, soul, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaWzjCwFlI?t=676
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Soul is not directly feeling anything in this world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that those seeking ultimate truth understand that experiences are generated by the material body, mind, and intelligence, while the soul merely observes and identifies with these feelings.

That is the verdict of the scriptures. But ultimately, those who want to go out of this material world, those who want ultimate truth, absolute truth, they understand that all these things are created by this body, which is body, mind and intelligence, which is a material yantra. And all experiences are generated out of this yantra, and the soul is only a kshetrajna. Soul is not feeling anything. Soul is feeling the feeling. Soul is not directly feeling anything in this world. When you feel angry with someone, you are not angry. Your kshetra is generating that anger and you are identifying with that anger. It is not direct feeling. The feeling of the yantra, you are identifying. Thinking of the yantra, you are identifying. Impulses of the yantra, you are misunderstanding to be your decision, your impulses.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker compares Maya to an expert jeweler who duplicates the gold of the spiritual world, creating illusions that deceive people into identifying with the body instead of the soul.

But the nature of the soul is to feel, to think, feel and will. Body cannot think, feel and will. But at the same time, maya is so powerful, she creates an illusion of thinking, feeling and willing. Just like the jeweler is so expert, that he plates it and makes it appear like gold. Copper, he will make it appear like gold. So that all the customers will go and buy it, pay the price of gold and buy it. Foolish customers will go, pay the price of gold and buy it. In the same way, in this material world, maya is expert, expert like a jeweler who cheats. She is expert. Her job is to duplicate the gold of the spiritual world here. Her business is to duplicate beauty here. Just like rolled gold will be only skin deep, isn't it? If you bend that copper like this, suddenly inside you will see copper. So it is little micron thickness. So the truth of that gold is only micron thickness, isn't it? Similarly, the shastra says that all beauties in this world is only skin deep.

Two legs and this and that and chest and ribs and everything. And then now some moving items are there. Okay, kidney I will hang here. Heart I will fit here, put one belt here. Exactly like that only, everything is hanging. Heart is hanging, one liver is hanging, then all connections are given. Then pumping system is there, blood is given. All this is going on. Yantra rudhani maya, it is yantra. But how many times do we think it is an yantra?

**Summary:** Using the analogy of mannequins in saree shops, the speaker explains how Maya triggers desires and illusions in our minds, leading us to identify with the body.

Just like in the saree shop outside you have mannequins. Fiberglass model of women and they wear saree. They decorate that mannequin with saree. Those who have desire, they see if people don't, if people see it as plastic. You think anybody, anybody will set it up outside the shop. Outside the shop. Suppose everybody is knowledgeable. Plastic. Those who have desire, they will see that model wearing the saree. They will project their own face there, how nice I will be there. And then they will enter the saree shop. Because this is how it is. We create in our mind illusion. And that mannequin, that fiberglass doll is just a trigger in your mind. You are just waiting to see how you will feel in that shanee. So in the material world, varieties of names and forms and qualities and persons and everything is duplicated by maya. But the person, where is the person? The person is in the body. You don't even see the person. The person is travelling in the body. But you paste, we paste the identity of the person onto the body. We ourselves first paste our identity that I am this body, and we continue going on life like this. Right from childhood we are grown, slowly we start identifying how.

So actually we are living in bodily concept of life. We are not living in the body. If you are living in the body then you become immediately conscious. Oh, what is this? This is a yantra. But you live in the bodily concept of life. Krishna consciousness is meant to take us out of this bodily concept of life and come into spiritual concept of life. I am spirit soul. To start with. One should see, separate the body. A body is simply neutral. Body has actually no consciousness. Consciousness is separate, body is separate. There is no difference between the body and the floor in which you are sitting. It is just an extension of pancha bhuta, that's all. That much pancha bhuta in your body, which is your body, you have control, that's all. But there is no difference on the floor. Because floor also doesn't talk, body also doesn't talk. Isn't it? Floor also doesn't think, your body also doesn't think. Floor also doesn't feel, your body also doesn't feel. The soul in the body thinks, the soul in the body feels, the soul in the body wills. But because we are in bodily concept of life.

My soul's thinking is influenced by this yantra. This is very nicely set up pancha bhuta, which duplicates, like a person. Because you see, if you want to duplicate gold ornaments, by just giving a layer of gold, you have to be very expert. Everything that appears there in the real gold should also appear here. All quality should appear here. So that's why maya has created this body, so all quality should appear. What is the quality of a soul? Thinking, feeling and willing. So you think you are the body, and the body is thinking, feeling and willing. I am this body, I am a man, I am a woman. So my moment I say I am a woman, my feeling, my identification, everything is colored of being a woman. I am a man. Immediately your consciousness is colored as a man. So the attraction in the material world, if you see the attraction between male and female, that's the subject that is here, the attraction between male and female has no sound basis in the spiritual platform. Why? Because to say that I am a male is first illusion, to say that I am a female is another illusion. So it is founded on illusory self-concept.

For them it's, you know, no form, no fame, no qualities for the body, for the person has no qualities, no thinking, no feeling, no willing. ### Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.13-14 | 27-09-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Vedic marriage, Respect, Culture vs. Wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, in offering one's daughter to a person, the culture and quality are counted as prominent, not wealth or any other material considerations."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the traditional Vedic marriage procedure where the father offers his daughter to a suitable boy, emphasizing the importance of culture and quality over material wealth.

The general procedure for Vedic marriage is that a father offers his daughter to a suitable boy. That is a very respectable marriage. A boy should not go to the girl's father and ask for the hand of his daughter in marriage. That is considered to be a humbling of one's respectable position. Swaimbhuvamanu wanted to convince Kardvamuni, since he knew that the sage wanted to marry a suitable girl. I am offering just such a suitable wife. Do not reject the offer, or else, because you are in need of a wife, you will have to ask for such a wife from someone else, who may not behave with you so well. In that case, your position will be humbled. Another feature of this incident is that Swaimbhuvamanu was the emperor, but he went to offer his qualified daughter to a poor brahmana. Kardvamuni had no worldly possessions, he was a hermit living in the forest, but he was advanced in culture. Therefore, in offering one's daughter to a person, the culture and quality are counted as prominent, not wealth or any other material considerations. I will read the next verse also.

---
#### Brahmacharya, Celibacy, Spiritual Orders
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari."
**Summary:** This segment explains the concept of brahmacharya, defining the two types: naishtika (lifelong celibacy) and upakurvanah (celibacy up to a certain age), and its importance in student life and spiritual orders.

aham tvashenavam vidvan vivahartam samuddhi adhyatam atastam upakurvanah pratham pratigrahaname. Swaimbhuvamanu continued, O wise man, I heard that you were prepared to marry. Please accept her hand which is being offered to you by me, since you have not taken a vow of perpetual celibacy. The principle of brahmacharya is celibacy. There are two kinds of brahmacharis. One is called naishtika brahmachari, which means one who takes a vow of celibacy for his whole life, whereas the other, upakurvanah brahmachari, or a brahmachari who takes the vow of celibacy up to a certain age. For example, he may take the vow to remain celibate up to 25 years of age, then with the permission of his spiritual master, he enters married life. Brahmacharya is student life, the beginning of life in the spiritual orders, so brahmacharya is celibacy. Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari.

---
#### Marriage, Karma, Respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And all these logic, they are all just like one who rejects an offering that comes of its own accord, but later begs a boon from a miser, loses his widespread reputation, and his pride is humbled by the neglectful behavior of others."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Swaimbhuvamanu's request to Kardvamuni to accept his daughter in marriage, highlighting the reasons and potential consequences of rejecting such an offer, relating it to the intricacies of karma.

Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari. Swaimbhuvamanu requested Garudvamuni to accept his daughter, and since Garudvama had not taken the vow of naishtika brahmachari, he was willing to marry the suitable daughter of a high royal family was presented. He was willing to marry, and the suitable daughter of a high royal family was presented. So Swaimbhuvamanu is requesting Garudvamuni with various reasons, why he should accept his offer, the offer of giving his daughter in marriage to Garudvamuni. So various reasons are being quoted by Swaimbhuvamanu. Different logic is being put forth to Garudvamuni. And all these logic, they are all just like one who rejects an offering that comes of its own accord, but later begs a boon from a miser, loses his widespread reputation, and his pride is humbled by the neglectful behavior of others. These are the intricacies of the laws of karma.

---
#### Ashrama, Personal Choice, Spiritual Advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=263
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is nothing absolute as far as the material condition of the living entity is concerned."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the choice of ashrama (brahmacharya, grahastha, etc.) is a personal decision based on internal reasoning and knowledge, not social pressure, and that there is nothing absolute about it.

So, not that Garudvamuni has to, you know, get into this wedlock, because of those reasons, because he was an elevated person, and he was a muni, a brahmana, a hermit living in the forest, and Prabhupada says that he was advanced in culture, so therefore the king was offering his daughter to him. Somehow or the other, by will of providence, Garudvamuni has also decided to become a grahastha. So there is nothing absolute like, you know, one should not become grahastha, one should remain brahmacharya, etc. There is nothing absolute as far as the material condition of the living entity is concerned. He can be in any varna, he can be in any ashrama, but it is up to the person to make a decision how he should, what ashrama he should choose, etc. He should have good reasons. Reasons means with knowledge. Not social reasons, not prestige reasons. Oh, I will be a brahmacharya, I will be a sannyasi, that will be great. Not all those things, for one's own internal reasoning. Not for the society thing, for his own internal reasoning. He may decide either to become grahastha or not become grahastha, remain a brahmacharya.

---
#### Krishna Consciousness, Materialism, Spiritual Expansion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CW5cb-l9fBI?t=366
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The principle is not to acquire things, not to create possessions. Not to have a luxurious lifestyle."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Prabhupada's approach to admitting grahasthas into ashramas, contrasting it with Gaudiya Math, and emphasizes the importance of minimizing material possessions and focusing on internal spiritual expansion in Krishna consciousness.

If one is very serious about chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, serious about spiritual life, seriously dedicated to Prabhupada's mission, then Prabhupada himself set the precedence of admitting grahasthas into the ashramas, unlike Gaudiya Math, where no grahasthas were admitted into the ashramas. So he created a system and Prabhupada said that if you can live like a brahmana, husband and wife, then you can leave off the temple. But if you want to live like a regular grahastha, have all the, you know, what the shastra says, how a regular grahastha should be, then he should go out, earn money and be that. That also does not stop. If the person is chanting Hare Krishna, that also does not stop. But these are all uphill tasks. To be in the midst of opulence, to be in the midst of acquiring so many things, definitely the quality of one's chanting, quality of one's advancement will come down. So devotees should, whoever has taken this path of Krishna consciousness should understand that it is a path of ultimately catering to the minimum needs of the body and the mind, whether one is a brahmacharya, grahastha, whatever it is. That is the principle. The principle is not to acquire things, not to create possessions. Not to have a luxurious lifestyle. These are all the principles, irrespective of what the ashrama is. Because once a devotee has accepted the path of Krishna consciousness, what is his decision? His decision is maintain my body and soul, my society, connections, all these things to the minimum. Certain things are required, I should maintain those minimum things. And where is my expansion? My expansion is internal. My expansion is to move forward in Krishna consciousness. My expansion is in understanding transcendental knowledge. My expansion is in understanding very clearly difference between matter and spirit. And after understanding, I can move forward.

### Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.15-16 | 04-10-2016
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### marriage, female psychology, religious regulations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m4tGbKgmLvw?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That is a great consideration because female psychology dictates that when a woman offers her heart to a man for the first time, it is very difficult for her to take it back."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Kardamamuni's considerations for accepting Devahuti as his wife, emphasizing her devotion and the importance of a first-class marriage.

There were many considerations by Kardamamuni before accepting the daughter of Swayambhuva Manu. Most important is that Devahuti had first of all fixed her mind on marrying him. She did not choose to have any other man as her husband. That is a great consideration because female psychology dictates that when a woman offers her heart to a man for the first time, it is very difficult for her to take it back. Also, she had not married before. She was a virgin girl. All these considerations convinced Kardamamuni to accept her. Therefore, he said, "Yes, I shall accept your daughter under religious regulations of marriage." There are different kinds of marriages of which the first class marriage is held by inviting a suitable bridegroom for the daughter and giving her in charity, well-dressed and well-decorated with ornaments along with the dowry according to the means of the father. There are other kinds of marriage such as Gandharva marriage and marriage by love which are also accepted as marriage. Even if one is forcibly kidnapped and later on accepted as a wife, that is also accepted. But Kardamamuni accepted the first-class way of marriage because the father was willing and the daughter was qualified. She had never offered her heart to anyone else. All these considerations made Kardamamuni agree to accept the daughter of Swaibuwa Manu.

---
#### social order, Kali Yuga, degradation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m4tGbKgmLvw?t=295
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the age of Kali, everyone will be like Shudras. The traditional social customs are not followed in this age, although formerly they were followed strictly."
**Summary:** This segment explains how the traditional Kshatriya principles have been abolished due to India's dependence on foreign countries, leading to a loss of social order and everyone being considered a Shudra in the age of Kali.

Afterward, an adjustment was made by the elderly members of the Kuru family. Such marriages were current in bygone ages, but at the present moment they are impossible because the strict principles of Kshatriya life have practically been abolished. Since India has become dependent on foreign countries, the particular influences of our social orders have been lost. Now, according to the scriptures, everyone is a Shudra. The so-called Brahmana, Kshatriya and Vaishyas have forgotten their traditional activities and the absence of these activities, they are called Shudras. It is said in the scriptures, "In the age of Kali, everyone will be like Shudras." The traditional social customs are not followed in this age, although formerly they were followed strictly. Thus in the Bhaktivedanta Parpat.

---
#### Varnashrama, cultural decline, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m4tGbKgmLvw?t=530
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As per the way life should be on earth, as created by the Lord, there is a social system by which the society conducts itself. The members in the society conduct themselves according to their varna and ashrama, which absolutely is non-existent now, even in this country."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the decline of the Varnashrama system and its impact on society, emphasizing the absence of traditional social structures and the need for a solution for Krishna conscious devotees.

So these activities of personalities like Kardamuni, Swayambhuva Manu, how they got married, all these things are described in Chimalbhagavatam. About her beauty, about the system in which one gets married, what is the right system. All these things, after probably another hundred years, will not be even, no traces of this even would be existing, even in this country. As it is also, when you read these things, you will see that the modern world is completely shaped into some other culture. As Prabhupada says, that is, the foreign countries, we became dependent. So India has become dependent on foreign countries. Particular influences of our social orders have been lost. So as per the way life should be on earth, as created by the Lord, there is a social system by which the society conducts itself. The members in the society conduct themselves according to their varna and ashrama, which absolutely is non-existent now, even in this country. Namesake, some people may call themselves brahmanas, etc. But otherwise, it doesn't exist at all. So when these things don't exist, when basically brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, shudra, and, you know, the four ashramas, when they do not exist at all, following the rules and regulations of those particular social system does not make sense at all.
