#### Satchidananda Nama, Mindful Japa, Analogy of Fire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TG64XRHZE5o?t=1121
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mindless Japa is like standing near the fire. Thousand rounds of Mindless Japa is equal to one round of Mindful Japa."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of iron and fire to illustrate the difference between mindless and mindful Japa, stating that mindless Japa is like standing near the fire, while mindful Japa is like putting the iron in the fire.

So that Satchidananda Nama has to be, when you start hearing, then it is Mindful Japa. Not hearing means Nama is before you, but there is a distance between your heart and the Nama. Just like if you hold a piece of iron outside the fire, near the fire, you will get some heat. If you put the iron in the fire, you will become red hot. So Mindless Japa is like standing near the fire. There is no comparison. Standing outside the fire and inside the fire, big difference. Similarly, thousand rounds of hundred or thousand rounds of Mindless Japa is equal to one round of Mindful Japa. Because if you keep outside, you may little more than room temperature, the iron piece will get heated. Inside it goes, it becomes red hot. Difference is not little between Mindless and Mindful Japa.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, who kindly descends into this material world, in the chants that I utter, as the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, which is Shuddhanama's Mantra Murti."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna descends into the material world as the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, which is Shuddhanama's Mantra Murti, and that this is more accessible than Archa Murti due to its merciful incarnation.

So, who kindly descends into this material world, in the chants that I utter, as the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, which is Shuddhanama's Mantra Murti. Just like Archa Vigraha, that you see with your eyes, is the Archa Murti of Krishna's Satchidananda Rupa. Similarly, this Mantra Murti, is the, it is the, this Shakti Avesha Nama, is the Mantra Murti of the Shuddhanama. Because we cannot chant and hear Shuddhanama, with our material tongue and material ears, therefore the Lord appears in the Mantra Murti. Because we cannot see the Lord, with our material eyes, therefore the Lord comes as Archa Murti. So, Shabda Mantra Murti, is more accessible to us than, Archa Murti. So, the very merciful incarnation. Now, this Nama Shabda, Shuddhanama Shabdas, the Shakti Avesha Nama Shabda, the Nama Avatara, is the crown jewel of all Veda Shabdas, whose toes are worshipped by the brilliant, spiritual, radiant, gem-like verses of Upanishads. The Upanishad Shabda, they all worship this, Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, as the supreme, because, the purpose of all the Upanishads, is to show us God. And here He is, Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, who has most kindly descended, through the Parampara, into the sounds that utter. We are chanting

These different people, different things, different situations, they are all characterized by one common character, one common quality, that they are all limited. #### spiritual senses, Krishna's kindness, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E2I2z2tu5sM?t=473
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is so kind, knowing our limitation to touch him transcendentally, He takes Avatara in the sound that we chant."
**Summary:** Because our spiritual senses are dormant, Krishna appears in the sound of the holy name, allowing us to serve Him through our senses and consciousness.

But because now our spiritual senses are all sleeping, our original consciousness is also sleeping. Therefore even though he is there within this sound, it is only by faith that we have to feel his presence. We cannot feel the presence of Shuddha Nama with our tongue, with our ears now. Krishna is so kind, knowing our limitation to touch him transcendentally. He takes Avatara in the sound that we chant, which is full of all the Shaktis of the Lord, the Chittananda Shakti. And through this Shakti Avesha Nama, the Shuddha Nama, Krishna receives our service of engaging our sense tongue, engaging our mind, engaging our ears to serve him with our consciousness. Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, Man Mana Bhava Madbhakto. Madhyaji Maam Namaskaru, Mame Vishnu Satyam Dev, Pratijnani Piyo Simhi. The biggest secret is to always remember Krishna, Man Mana Bhava Madbhakto. Keep Krishna always in one's mind, in one's Chittam, consciousness as the greatest truth. And that truth we can easily practice by always keeping Krishna on our tongue, ears and mind. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. That is the biggest secret. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu taught us how to practice Man Mana Bhava Madbhakto.

### Why Mechanical Japa Block Spiritual Growth | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | Ekadashi Soulful Japa Training
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Nama Avatara, Shuddha Nama, Shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So what is before us when we chant the holy names of the Lord is Krishna Himself."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of Nama Avatara, stating that the holy name of Krishna is non-different from Krishna Himself and is imbued with all His shaktis.

He descends on our tongue in the sound that we utter as the Shakti Avesha Nama, Naam Naam Kaari Baudha Nija Sarva Shakti. All shaktis of Krishna enter the sound we utter. And the sound we utter is no longer a material sound. All the shaktis of the Lord have entered. These shaktis are the effulgence of the Shuddha Nama of the spiritual world. So therefore we call this as Nama Avathara. The name of the Shuddha Nama is Nama Avathara. The name of the Supreme Lord incarnates into the names that we utter. He invests all his shaktis into the name. So what is before us when we chant the holy names of the Lord is Krishna Himself. Because Krishna and Krishna's name are non-different. Just like Krishna only becomes His name, and His name is Shuddha Nama. Just like Krishna is all effulgent, sat-Chidanananda effulgence, similarly Krishna. Similarly, Krishna's namah, Shuddhanama, is also Surya, Son of sat-Chidanananda effulgence, Chittananda effulgence. Sat Chittananda, these energies enter the sound that we utter. So before us the Shakti Avesha Nama and the Shuddha Nama. Shuddha Nama's incarnation into this world is the Shakti Avesha Nama. He is incarnating into the sound that you and me utter. So it's called Shabdha Avatara. Nama Avatara of the Shuddha Nama is before us, which we can hear with our material tongue.

---
#### Satchitananda, Holy Name, Association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=265
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So by receiving the causeless mercy of the Lord in the form of the Holy Names into which He invests all His Shakti, the Shuddha Nama and His effulgence are non-different from each other."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the Satchitananda nature of the holy name and how chanting attentively allows one to associate with this pure energy, burning away material coverings.

So by receiving the causeless mercy of the Lord in the form of the Holy Names into which He invests all His Shakti, Naam Naam Kaari Baudha Nija Sarva Shakti, the Shuddha Nama and His effulgence are non-different from each other. So His effulgence enters this sound, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So this Satchitananda energy that is there in the names that we utter, when our attention touches or when we hear these names, then we are touching the ocean of Satchitananda. Just like Prabhupada gives the example of how when an iron piece is put in the fire, the iron by association of the fire, the fire's energy heat is passed on to the iron piece and the iron gradually becomes warmer and warmer and warmer and finally it becomes red hot fire-like by transfer of energy. Similarly, by chanting and hearing, attentively, the Shakti Avesha Rama, our consciousness also gets Satchitanandaized by flow of Sat energy, Chit energy and Ananda energy. And the covering of this present soul, our present condition, the covering is Asat, Achit and Nirananda. So the Sat, Chit and Ananda energy that is coming from the Shakti Avesha Rama, the fire of Satchitananda Rama, Shakti Avesha Rama, will burn to ashes Asat, Achit and Nirananda. Because of these three coverings, we have forgotten who we are, what is our relationship with Krishna, we have forgotten what is real Ananda because of this covering. And the Lord is so kind and merciful that He comes down to give us His Satchitananda association. Just like Sun is surrounded by the Sun, the Sun is surrounded by light and heat, so much of effulgence. Similarly, Krishna is Satchitananda Vigraha. His name is Satchitananda Vigraha and they are like the Sun from whom unlimited Satchitananda effulgence is emanating. So associating with Nama means associating with the Sat, Chit and Ananda energy, pure energies.

---
#### Nama Chintamani, Mind, Hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=459
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only when you hear, the mind becomes pure."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the holy name as Nama Chintamani, a fulfiller of all desires, and emphasizes the importance of attentive hearing in purifying the mind and connecting with the Lord.

So Nama Chintamani, Krishna Chaitanya Rasa Vigraha. So the Nama is Chintamani, a fulfiller of all desires. Krishna Chaitanya Rasa Vigraha. This sound is a vigraha of all rasas. Krishna Chaitanya Rasa Vigraha. This sound is just oozing all pleasure, all kinds of relationship with Krishna is a reservoir. Rasa Vigraha, poornashuddha nithyamukta poornashuddha means completely pure. That means there is no Asatha Chitnirananda. Purnashuddha nithyamukta. Life is finite to all forms that matter. That is why Raara is must. Ananda Incorporated with Nama. Yoga prostmens himself to all many forms of birth and death where there is no Prana, no river, no stream of water. Ananda Hrib seat else. Ananda Hrib antena puush with this. Coming here with Matthashiva, if you are a defector or anything you should keep it away. But look at the shot because here there is no eternally transcendental, beyond the three modes of material nature, abhinatva nama naminoha. There is no difference between the nama and the one whose name it is. So the Lord through the nama avatara gives us association. And what does association mean? Association means just like iron put in the fire, there is a flow of fire from the fire to the iron, flow of heat. In the same way, there is a flow of Satchitananda provided we hear what we chant. By hearing, by hearing we need to give the mind. Only when you hear, the mind becomes pure. Mind means chittam. Mind means manas, buddhi, ahankara. Three layers. Ego, intelligence, mind. They all have to... Asat, achitta and nirananda are sitting in these three places. And when we attentively hear, this shakti avesha nama is touching all these three. When you hear, mind is required. When you chant, your ego is required. I am chanting. I am hearing. So in this way, attentive hearing brings tremendous difference to this self.

#### Kali Yuga, Nama Avatara, Personal Relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=653
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord has come for me."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the fortunate nature of the Nama Avatara in Kali Yuga and the importance of developing a personal relationship with the Lord through the holy name.

Just like Prabhupada gives example, if you put hand in the fire, child or no, adult or anybody, it will burn. In the same way, very fortunate that the Lord has decided in Kali Yuga to descend in His holy names. So it is... That is called nama avatara. Should take advantage of this. Avatara means Lord has become accessible to us in the form of this secret we should understand. If you understand this secret, from this you can derive everything else. You will know why you have to be attentive. You know why you have to be respectful. You know why you have to be joyfully chant. Lord Himself is before me. If that fundamental shraddha is developed, for shraddha also, these are all the wonderful analogies that the acharyas have given in order to build our shraddha. That Lord is before me when I chant and He is before each one of us personally. It is not that the Lord does not care. The Supreme Lord cares for every amsa, every one of us. So you have to understand that the Lord is very personal when He comes on our tongue. But we are impersonal. So we should also be personal. The Lord has come for me. He comes for everybody. That's a different matter. But the fact remains that if He comes for everybody, He doesn't become diluted in His personal appearance for each one of us.

#### Personal Relationship, Causeless Mercy, Nama Tattva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=783
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you understand this Nama Tattva, what is actually happening, you are touching the Supreme Lord Krishna in His Nama Avatara, who is supremely pure."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Krishna's personal relationship with each individual to that of a father with many children, and highlights the causeless mercy that allows some to serve the Holy Name.

A big man comes or a father is having ten children. His attention is divided into ten children. Is it not? Even though, they are all my children, He says. But Krishna is not like that, the Supreme Father. Though everyone has a direct relationship with Him, in a general sense, oh, they are all my... mamaya vamsa jiva loke jiva bhuta sanatana all are your amsas, my amsas. He doesn't leave it at that. He has a direct relationship with every living entity. So we have... That is the reason why He has come on our tongue. No doubt, some people get benefit of this avatara. Some people don't get the benefit of this avatara. That is due to the causeless mercy of the spiritual masters, Srila Prabhupada, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's causeless mercy that some of us get this opportunity. Everybody can get, but some of us have already got this opportunity. Where we begin to serve the Holy Name, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. And some are even more fortunate. If you understand this Nama Tattva, what is actually happening, who is this sound, what is this sound, what happens when you chant, you are touching the Supreme Lord Krishna in His Nama Avatara, who is supremely pure, Pavitram. So even more fortunate. But this is, this transcendental understanding of what is going on during japa, what is going on in the world, the Lord descends into the sound. It is very important to have this knowledge in the background.

The understanding is required in the background. How does this understanding come? You have to repeatedly, you have to think about it. You have to do mananam. There has to be on these simple things, what few things that just now you heard. When you hear, everything feels good for you, yeah, I understood it. But it has to become part of your moment, you utter Hare Krishna. This perception has to come, that He is before me. So for that, why He is before me? All these things is what I explained. You should do mananam. Yeah, yeah, I can understand. I can believe. I don't qualify, but He is so all-merciful, He can come. And the shastra says He comes. So He comes, so He is there. I have shraddha, I have faith. That, many times you should think, because your conscious mind is fine, you understand that. But you have a subconscious mind, which has its own ignorance. This has to go inside there. Because normally when we hear any sound, it is ordinary sound. Now if our default perception has to change, that this is not ordinary sound, it is Krishna, you have to do mananam. Many times you have to tell your subconscious mind, again and again, this is the truth, this is the truth, this is the truth.

#### Mananam, Dhyanam, Emotional Contemplation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1050
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you do dhyanam, it is emotional contemplation of the factual relationship between me and Nama Tattva, Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between Mananam (rational reflection) and Dhyanam (emotional contemplation), emphasizing the need to emotionalize one's relationship with Krishna.

Not only mananam, mananam is intellectually, you are making your belief stronger and stronger. We also have to do dhyanam. Dhyanam. Mananam is, what is truth. Dhyanam is, what is your relationship with that truth. Krishna comes for me. Yes, I belong to Krishna. Krishna is so kind. All these, when you do dhyanam, it is emotional contemplation. When you do mananam, it is the rational reflection, repeated rational reflection of the truth about the Nama Tattva. When you do dhyanam, it is emotional contemplation of the factual relationship between me and Nama Tattva, Nama Prabhu. Though we say, yes, I am eternal servant of Krishna, but it is not emotionalized. It is only philosophy. So, it is very important that, that emotional contemplation also is required. Emotional contemplation of relating with Nama Prabhu.

#### Emotional Contemplation, Shravanam, Mananam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1166
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For any relationship to become deepened, first is emotional contemplation."
**Summary:** The speaker uses analogies of marriage and a new job to illustrate the importance of emotional contemplation in deepening relationships, and connects it to Shravanam and Mananam.

Just like you see, someone is, man and woman are fixed for wedding, to be wedded. Then, they are in emotional contemplation. Imagine, living together. Relationship, nice, love, this, everything. Isn't it? Emotional contemplation. Or, you have got an appointment letter for a good job, very good job, big money, big salary. So, you are not enjoying the job, but you indulge in emotional contemplation. Oh, how nice it will be, my future, when I get this salary, when I get this, all these things. Emotional contemplation. Similarly, we should also have, for any relationship to become deepened, first is emotional contemplation. First is the truth about the relationship. That is rational reflection. That is coming from Shravanam. Shravanam, first you hear, and then, you, what you heard, you engrave in your subconscious, that is called Mananam. First you hear, Krishna has come down, Krishna is this, Krishna is that, no difference between His sound and Himself, all the Shaktis are entering, this is Shakti-Vajanama, the Lord is before me, but I cannot taste Him, I cannot experience Him, but I can experience His Shaktis, etc., etc., etc., all these things, you hear, what I said. In summary, Namatattva, you hear. Shravanam.

#### Shravanam, Mananam, Dhyanam, Anushilanam, Soulful Japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1286
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you chant, with right belief, about Nama Prabhu, and feeling for Nama Prabhu, that becomes soulful Japa."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Anushilanam (practice) as the next step after Shravanam, Mananam, and Dhyanam, and defines soulful Japa as chanting with the right belief and feeling for Nama Prabhu.

Now, if you don't do Mananam, it will just go away. Or a hundred times, you hear, different, different times, slowly, it deficits into your subconscious mind. So, you have to do Mananam. And then, Dhyanam. This is not during Japa. But, then there is one more step. Just like I gave example, then, when you actually get into the job, a good job you have got, then not all emotionally, emotional contemplation, with full belief and everything, you are now actually engaging with that experience. Earlier, you were contemplating that experience. Next, you are engaging with that experience. You started going for the job. You started getting the salary. You got, getting, everybody in the, you know, community is praising you. Oh, now you are this, you are that. That, that is called Anushilanam. Anushilanam means, practice. That means, you have to practice that experience. So, Shravanam, Mananam, Dhyanam, Anushilanam. Anushilanam is what we are doing now. Practicing soulful Japa. Practicing. Practicing Japa, with Shraddha, faith, that background faith. Chanting, hearing, you are doing with your foreground. Chanting, hearing, tasking, moving the Mala, increasing the number of beads, and Japa, towards the target. That is tasking. Chanting, hearing, tasking is the activity. Three are activities. Action has to be backed with feelings. Thought feelings. Bhavana. Belief and feelings. So, when you chant, with right belief, about Nama Prabhu, and feeling for Nama Prabhu, that becomes soulful Japa.

#### Soulful Japa, Shravanam, Relationship Ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1429
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every beat will be a relationship with the Nama."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a background consciousness grounded in Shravanam is essential for soulful Japa, and that practicing Japa develops a relationship ego with the Holy Name.

So, not that we keep our background consciousness empty. Of course, it will be empty, if you have not done Mananam, Dhyanam. Actually, if you go on repeatedly hearing automatically Mananam, Dhyanam will happen. Mananam is stage two of Shravanam. Dhyanam is stage one of them. Stage two is Dhyanam. And then you practice that stage three. So, everything comes from Shravanam. Your consciousness is grounded with Shravanam. And we hear from Guru, Sadhu and Shastra, all the narratives. The narratives from Guru, Sadhu and Shastra, is what creates our perceptions about the world, about the thing, about the people, about Nama, about Krishna, everything. So, somewhere we have to start. Then what happens? When you Anushilanam, when you practice it, then you develop a relationship ego. I am servant of a realistic relationship ego, that I am servant of Nama Prabhu. I am chanter, I am hearer, I am tasker, I am serving. So, once that relationship is there, that is what, as soon as you take your beat back, your relationship persona with the Holy Name, along with its Shraddha, Preeti, everything that you have developed, that will automatically come. So, then every beat will be a relationship with the Nama.

#### Relationship, Mechanical Japa, Nama Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1558
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "50 years also you do mechanical japa, you won't be closer to the Nama than to any of your friends."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a deepening marital relationship to illustrate the lack of progress in chanting due to mechanical japa, and emphasizes the need to develop the belief that the sound is Krishna.

See, just like I asked in one seminar, Japa seminar, how many of you are married? Many of them raised their head. How many years? They said five years, six years, three years, twenty years, everything. Then I asked them, is there a difference in your relationship between both of you from the beginning of first day of your marriage to second year, third year, fourth year, fifth year, sixth year? Isn't it becoming deeper and deeper relationship? Everybody said, yes, definitely. I never knew her, he never knew, I never knew him. But, after few months, relationship, relationship, it is deepening. Then I asked, how many years you are chanting Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. They all said, two years, three years, five years, six years, seven years. Hare, with your wife, you are able to build relationship. But with Krishna himself, with everyday, two hours you are with. Maybe in your real life, you are not even, you are going to office, coming back, maybe you talk with him. Still you build a relationship. But here you are not building relationship. Why? So, first of all, you are not feeling he is a person. So, step, mananam, had to develop the belief that this sound is Krishna. then it will change because Krishna has said I have come down I am part of your life by the mercy of Prabhupada who has given us two hours association spend two hours every day so our realization of Nama Prabhu our realization of everything should only deepen day by day the reason why it is not happening is because we are doing mechanical japa 50 years also you do mechanical japa you won't be closer to the Nama than to any of your friends there is a realistic relationship personal relationship Lord has come for us to relate with us so therefore this practice is important.

#### Soulful Japa, Shravanam, Mananam, Dhyanam, Nama Tattva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1749
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Be determined to understand the Nama Tattva, develop the belief that who the sound is before you, it's all one time investment, it goes into your system."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the practice of soulful japa is important and that Shravanam, Mananam, and Dhyanam are one-time efforts that create the necessary background for engaging with Nama Prabhu.

This practice of soulful japa when we are seeing these cards on the screen during japa that is only last part anushilanam we can go on practicing increasing the quality of japa but shravanam mananam dhyanam is one time effort that we have not put be determined to understand the Nama Tattva develop the belief that who the sound is before you it's all one time investment it goes into your system then afterwards you don't have to go on doing that anushilanam practice you can go on because you are engaging with Nama Prabhu eternally you are engaging but to engage with Nama Prabhu you have to understand you have to have the background created by shravanam mananam and dhyanam of the Nama Tattva our relationship with Nama what all guru, sadhu and shastra has said about Nama that you have to become rich when our background is rich then our foreground activity becomes very rich in relationship. Background consciousness during japa is very important.

#### Background Consciousness, Nama Tattva, Guru, Sadhu, Shastra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=1854
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our background consciousness should become rich with this Nama Tattva."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of an air-conditioned room to illustrate how a rich background consciousness enhances the quality of chanting and hearing, and emphasizes the role of Guru, Sadhu, and Shastra in feeding this consciousness.

It increases the quality of our chanting and hearing. Just like if you are sitting in an air-conditioned room, it is a background. You are sitting and working. A good air-conditioned temperature-controlled room is going to increase the quality of your work or not, isn't it? So when we do chanting and hearing there is an internal environment. The environment consists of feelings, our faith, all these things. So we should our background consciousness should become rich with this Nama Tattva. What is Nama Tattva, what is Nama Rasa, all these things has to be fed from Guru, Sadhu and Shastra starting with Sravana.

#### Relationship, Detachment, Mercy

Then you will see your quality of japa will change and as you practice no doubt a concrete relationship will be developed with Nama Avatara a concrete relationship he is there for you for everything he is there to guide you he is there to lead us back to home back to Godhead he is there to shine us shine into our heart powerful Divya Jnana which will burn all asat, chitta, nir-ananda beliefs that we have our heart is full of false beliefs of ananda of this material world all that has to be burnt and when you see that happening you will doubt hey, how is this happening how I am getting detached oh my god this is Nama Prabhu is doing it I am a different person now than I what I was before all my old ideas are all changing pure chitta is coming into my heart my beliefs about pleasure my beliefs about happiness my everything is changing from false beliefs to real belief what is real ananda real happiness everything will start coming into your heart shining by the rain of mercy of Satchidananda.

#### Japa Cards, Anusarana, Shraddha, Vinamrita Japa, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=2012
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For you to chant with humility, you have to have done lot of Shravanam, Mananam and Dhyanam, what is humility."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain the use of japa cards, starting with Anusarana (obedience), Shraddha (faith), and Vinamrita Japa (humility), emphasizing the need for prior Shravanam, Mananam, and Dhyanam to cultivate genuine humility.

So regarding these cards japa cards I will just explain four first put that common card sixteen put that common card sixteen where is Indrapati? one seven seven in two eight nine ten 12 three two three two one two three two three one two three two three two four two three one two three same sound. So, with that obedience, that in the background, Guru's direction, with a purpose and invoking Divya Chesta Shakti. When you hear, Prabhupada says, the Shakti for Japa comes from the Nama Himself. That is how we are all able to chant 108 rounds. Because the Shakti, the Chesta Shakti, Chesta Shakti means the doing energy is coming from Nama Himself. If you don't hear, you cannot chant 108 rounds. At least some, you are focusing that it should be heard. Because I am going on prompting now and then. And you know the whole Prabhupada himself, Prabhupada's recording itself comes and prompts you, chant and hear exactly the same sound. Therefore, you know, even every now and then some hearing is happening. That is why our, we are able to do 108 rounds. Shakti is coming from there. You don't realize that. Otherwise mind will say enough, enough, enough, enough, enough. Mind will say enough. It's because it's tiring. It becomes tiring. Shraddha Japa. Mala too is, your background environment is respectfully presencing. Shraddha Japa. Namaprabhu, before me with faith, feelings, Krishnam is before me. Krishna is before me. Krishna is before me. With that practice, second mala, you dedicate for Shraddha Japa. First mala, you dedicate for Anusarana. Means obedience to Prabhupada. On His command, I chant the way He says. So you start, chant. Second is Shraddha Japa. Third is Vinamrmudha Japa. For all human beings, Nama, Prabhu, before me with faith feelings. Krishna is before me. Krishna is before me. Krishna is before me, with that, practice. Second Mala, you dedicate for Shraddha Japa. This is Vinamrita Japa, chant with humility before Nama Prabhu. Now for you to chant with humility, you have to have done lot of Shravanam, Mananam and Dhyanam, what is humility. Otherwise it won't come out at that time. Humility is a feeling. A feeling if it has to generate, you have to have the thoughts, just like if you are angry with someone, there is a thought behind that anger. Similarly, if there is a feeling of humility, there has to be a thought behind it. And that thought behind it, philosophy behind it, you have to have hear how insignificant you are etc, etc, etc, all that thing, just see. You see the connection? So now whatever little you have got understanding of humility, you can chant today. But parallelly you can go on doing your Mananam. Shravanam, Dhyanam on all these things, your quality of humility will go on increasing. But today you can chant and hear only with the extent of humility that you have developed by Shravanam, Mananam and Dhyanam.

#### Preeti Japa, Atma Jnana Japa, Transcendental Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9jDvyYC8mUQ?t=2236
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to give your soul. Because soul is what, you know, wants relationship with Krishna, not your Yantra."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining the japa cards, covering Preeti Japa (love and pleasure), and Atma Jnana Japa (self-knowledge), emphasizing the importance of transcendental knowledge and detachment in devotional service.

Preeti Japa, feelings of love and pleasure giving to Nama Prabhu. In one sentence it is simple. But why? Because you feel love. When you know that someone loves you only, your love comes. That means all this, your Shravanam, actually Krishna loves us all. When that becomes a realization, automatic. So when he says, I am chanting and hearing, he thinks his body is chanting and hearing. But gradually, by the touch of the holy name itself, real self-knowledge, liberation of body and the self, all these things also in the background, your quality of Japa changes. When you understand that you are different from this body and you are using this body and offering this Seva, you are the real chanter and hearer. The Yantra Japa, mechanical Japa is called Yantra Japa. You are not involved. You are involved somewhere else. And you are offering Yantra Japa. That's also so powerful, such a big Punya it is. So powerful. But you don't get Bhakti if you do Yantra Japa. You have to give your soul. Because soul is what, you know, wants relationship with Krishna, not your Yantra. So that is Atma Jnana Japa. So entire, see for Atma Jnana, background who you are, who God is, why are you here, all these Jnana, when you read Prabhupada's book Shravanam, they are all matter, matters a lot in creating the background. Devotional service has to be executed in the background of transcendental knowledge and detachment. Something is required in the background. Knowledge, transcendental knowledge and detachment from this illusion. And then execution of service.
